revision.comment: Text replacement - "↵↵↵↵" to " "
revision.text:
[[File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, chumon-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins (National Historic Site)}}]] [[File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, chumon-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins (National Historic Site)}}]] {{座標一覧}} '''Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins''' (下野国分寺跡) are the remains of an ancient [[Buddhist temple]] in [[Shimotsuke City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
It is part of the network of [[provincial temples]] established across Japan by Emperor [[Shōmu]] during the [[Nara period]]. The area has been developed as a historic site park.
This article also discusses the '''Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins''', another national historic site. For information about the current temple, see '''{{ill|Shimotsuke Kokubunji|ja|下野国分寺|12=draft}}'''.
== Overview == The temple ruins are located on a plateau in western [[Shimotsuke City]], between the [[Sugata River]] and the [[Omoi River]] (栃木県). On the opposite bank of the Omoi River, the [[Shimotsuke Kokufu Ruins]] remain. The region, home to some of the largest [[keyhole-shaped tumuli]] (前方後円墳) in Tochigi Prefecture, such as the [[Azuma Kofun]], [[:en:Biwazuka Kofun|Biwazuka Kofun]], and [[Marishitenzuka Kofun]], was a base for the [[Shimotsukeno clan]] during ancient times.
The Kokubunji and Kokubun-niji Ruins are located approximately 600 meters apart from each other, oriented east to west. They have been preserved as part of the '''Tenpyō Hill Park''' (天平の丘公園) within the broader [[Fudoki Hill]] historic site area. The park features a variety of cherry trees, including offspring of the [[Three Great Cherry Trees of Japan]] ([[Jindai Zakura]], [[Usuzumi Zakura]], and [[Miharu Takizakura]]) and [[Ishiwari Zakura]], as well as many varieties of [[Yaezakura]]. During cherry blossom season, the area attracts large crowds of flower-viewers.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20220328132917/https://www.ana.co.jp/ja/jp/japan-travel-planner/tochigi/0000017.html Tenpyō Hill Park]. ANA.</ref>
The surrounding forest, mainly consisting of [[konara]] oak, [[kunugi]] oak, and [[zelkova]], represents a prime example of lowland forests in northern [[Kantō]], designated as the Kokubunji Greenery Environmental Conservation Area by Tochigi Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.tochigi.lg.jp/d04/30jikannti/09kokubunji.html|title=09 国分寺緑地環境保全地域|website=Tochigi Prefecture|accessdate=2024-11-11}}</ref>
== Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins == The temple site measures 457 meters north-south and 413 meters east-west.<ref name=shimotsuke>[http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002400/hpg000002382.htm Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins] (Shimotsuke City Website).</ref> The temple layout follows the Tōdaiji-style with the South Gate, Middle Gate, Golden Hall, and Lecture Hall aligned in a straight line from south to north. The Middle Gate and Golden Hall are connected by a cloister.<ref name=shimotsuke/> A seven-story pagoda, estimated from the size of its base, stood east of the cloister. Other features include bell towers and sutra repositories between the Golden Hall and Lecture Hall.<ref name=shimotsuke/>
Excavation revealed five distinct phases of development:<ref name=shimotsuke/> * Phase 1: Mid-8th century - Founding period, construction of the pagoda and Golden Hall. * Phase 2: Late 8th to early 9th century - Completion tier 2]][[Category: of major buildings, construction of a wooden-pillar fence around the temple grounds. * Phase 3: Late 9th century - Renovation period, replacement of the wooden-pillar fence with an earthen wall. * Phase 4: From the 10th century - Decline, cessation of repairs to main buildings and moat re-digging. * Phase 5: By the 11th-12th centuries, the temple’s flame of faith was extinguished.
The main features of the temple are: ; Golden Hall : Foundation: 21 meters north-south, 33.6 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 13.8 meters (4 bays) north-south, 25.8 meters (7 bays) east-west. ; Lecture Hall : Foundation: 16.2 meters north-south, 24.6 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 12.6 meters (4 bays) north-south, 21 meters (7 bays) east-west. ; Cloisters ; Bell Tower : Foundation: 12 meters north-south, 9 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 9 meters (3 bays) north-south, 6 meters (2 bays) east-west. ; Sutra Repository : Foundation: 12 meters north-south, 9 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 9 meters (3 bays) north-south, 6 meters (2 bays) east-west. ; Middle Gate : Foundation: 10.2 meters north-south, 22.8 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 2 bays north-south, 5 bays east-west. ; Pagoda : Foundation: 18 meters square. Building dimensions: 3 bays square, estimated height of over 60 meters. A reconstruction model is displayed in the Fudoki Hill Museum. ; South Gate
<gallery> File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato kondou.JPG|Golden Hall Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato koudou.JPG|Lecture Hall Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato soubou.JPG|Cloister Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato shourou.JPG|Bell Tower Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato kyouzou.JPG|Sutra Repository Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato chuumon.JPG|Middle Gate Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato tou.JPG|Pagoda Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato nandaimon.JPG|South Gate Ruins </gallery>
== Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins == Located approximately 600 meters east of the Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins. The temple site measures 270 meters north-south and 145 meters east-west, with a projecting section on the east side measuring 211 meters north-south and 52 meters east-west.<ref name=shimotsukeniji>[http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002500/hpg000002418.htm Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins] (Shimotsuke City Website).</ref> The layout is similar to that of Shimotsuke Kokubunji, but no pagoda was constructed.<ref name=shimotsukeniji/>
<gallery> File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, kondou.jpg|Golden Hall Ruins File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, koudou.jpg|Lecture Hall Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunniji-ato shourou.JPG|Bell Tower Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunniji-ato kyouzou.JPG|Sutra Repository Ruins File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, chumon-1.jpg|Middle Gate Ruins </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins - Designated on March 3, 1921, with additions to the designated area on March 2, 2005, and March 10, 2015.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|463|Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins}}</ref> * Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins - Designated on April 9, 1965, with an addition on July 28, 1977.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|485|Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}}</ref>
== Location == '''Addresses''' * Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins: {{Coord|36|23|06.21|N|139|48|21.95|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins}} * Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins: {{Coord|36|23|06.98|N|139|48|44.73|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|name=Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}}
'''Related Facilities''' * Tochigi Prefectural Fudoki Hill Museum ** Admission: 100 yen for adults, 50 yen for high school and university students ** Hours: 9:00 AM–5:00 PM (last admission at 4:30 PM) ** Closed: Mondays (open on holidays and Prefectural Day), the day after holidays (open on weekends), year-end and New Year holidays
'''Access''' * Train: [[East Japan Railway Company]] ([[JR East]]) [[Utsunomiya Line]] ([[Tōhoku Main Line]]) [[Koganei Station]] - Approximately 3.5 km ** Bicycle rentals available at the station
'''Nearby''' * {{ill|Shimotsuke Kokubunji|ja|下野国分寺|12=draft}} - Successor temple. * Kokubun Kofun Cluster - Includes {{ill|Kabutozuka Kofun|ja|甲塚古墳 (下野市)|12=draft}}, Sannōzuka Kofun, Atago Shrine Kofun, and Marutsuka Kofun. * [[:en:Shimotsuke_Provincial_Capital|Shimotsuke Provincial Capital]]
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== References == * On-site explanatory boards * {{Cite book|title=Historical Place Names of Tochigi Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|chapter=Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins and Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimotsuke Kokubunji}} {{Commonscat|Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins|下野国分尼寺跡}} * [http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002400/hpg000002382.htm Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins], [http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002500/hpg000002418.htm Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins] - Shimotsuke City Website * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|463}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|485|Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}} * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/1341002.htm Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins], [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/1341016.htm Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins] - Tochigi Cultural Assets * [http://shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=5 Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins], [http://shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=7 Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins] - Shimotsuke Cultural Heritage

The surrounding forest, mainly consisting of [[konara]] oak, [[kunugi]] oak, and [[zelkova]], represents a prime example of lowland forests in northern [[Kantō]], designated as the Kokubunji Greenery Environmental Conservation Area by Tochigi Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.tochigi.lg.jp/d04/30jikannti/09kokubunji.html|title=09 国分寺緑地環境保全地域|website=Tochigi Prefecture|accessdate=2024-11-11}}</ref>
== Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins == The temple site measures 457 meters north-south and 413 meters east-west.<ref name=shimotsuke>[http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002400/hpg000002382.htm Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins] (Shimotsuke City Website).</ref> The temple layout follows the Tōdaiji-style with the South Gate, Middle Gate, Golden Hall, and Lecture Hall aligned in a straight line from south to north. The Middle Gate and Golden Hall are connected by a cloister.<ref name=shimotsuke/> A seven-story pagoda, estimated from the size of its base, stood east of the cloister. Other features include bell towers and sutra repositories between the Golden Hall and Lecture Hall.<ref name=shimotsuke/>
Excavation revealed five distinct phases of development:<ref name=shimotsuke/> * Phase 1: Mid-8th century - Founding period, construction of the pagoda and Golden Hall. * Phase 2: Late 8th to early 9th century - Completion tier 2]][[Category: of major buildings, construction of a wooden-pillar fence around the temple grounds. * Phase 3: Late 9th century - Renovation period, replacement of the wooden-pillar fence with an earthen wall. * Phase 4: From the 10th century - Decline, cessation of repairs to main buildings and moat re-digging. * Phase 5: By the 11th-12th centuries, the temple’s flame of faith was extinguished.
The main features of the temple are: ; Golden Hall : Foundation: 21 meters north-south, 33.6 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 13.8 meters (4 bays) north-south, 25.8 meters (7 bays) east-west. ; Lecture Hall : Foundation: 16.2 meters north-south, 24.6 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 12.6 meters (4 bays) north-south, 21 meters (7 bays) east-west. ; Cloisters ; Bell Tower : Foundation: 12 meters north-south, 9 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 9 meters (3 bays) north-south, 6 meters (2 bays) east-west. ; Sutra Repository : Foundation: 12 meters north-south, 9 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 9 meters (3 bays) north-south, 6 meters (2 bays) east-west. ; Middle Gate : Foundation: 10.2 meters north-south, 22.8 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 2 bays north-south, 5 bays east-west. ; Pagoda : Foundation: 18 meters square. Building dimensions: 3 bays square, estimated height of over 60 meters. A reconstruction model is displayed in the Fudoki Hill Museum. ; South Gate
<gallery> File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato kondou.JPG|Golden Hall Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato koudou.JPG|Lecture Hall Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato soubou.JPG|Cloister Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato shourou.JPG|Bell Tower Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato kyouzou.JPG|Sutra Repository Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato chuumon.JPG|Middle Gate Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato tou.JPG|Pagoda Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato nandaimon.JPG|South Gate Ruins </gallery>
== Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins == Located approximately 600 meters east of the Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins. The temple site measures 270 meters north-south and 145 meters east-west, with a projecting section on the east side measuring 211 meters north-south and 52 meters east-west.<ref name=shimotsukeniji>[http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002500/hpg000002418.htm Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins] (Shimotsuke City Website).</ref> The layout is similar to that of Shimotsuke Kokubunji, but no pagoda was constructed.<ref name=shimotsukeniji/>
<gallery> File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, kondou.jpg|Golden Hall Ruins File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, koudou.jpg|Lecture Hall Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunniji-ato shourou.JPG|Bell Tower Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunniji-ato kyouzou.JPG|Sutra Repository Ruins File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, chumon-1.jpg|Middle Gate Ruins </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins - Designated on March 3, 1921, with additions to the designated area on March 2, 2005, and March 10, 2015.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|463|Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins}}</ref> * Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins - Designated on April 9, 1965, with an addition on July 28, 1977.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|485|Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}}</ref>
== Location == '''Addresses''' * Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins: {{Coord|36|23|06.21|N|139|48|21.95|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins}} * Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins: {{Coord|36|23|06.98|N|139|48|44.73|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|name=Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}}
'''Related Facilities''' * Tochigi Prefectural Fudoki Hill Museum ** Admission: 100 yen for adults, 50 yen for high school and university students ** Hours: 9:00 AM–5:00 PM (last admission at 4:30 PM) ** Closed: Mondays (open on holidays and Prefectural Day), the day after holidays (open on weekends), year-end and New Year holidays
'''Access''' * Train: [[East Japan Railway Company]] ([[JR East]]) [[Utsunomiya Line]] ([[Tōhoku Main Line]]) [[Koganei Station]] - Approximately 3.5 km ** Bicycle rentals available at the station
'''Nearby''' * {{ill|Shimotsuke Kokubunji|ja|下野国分寺|12=draft}} - Successor temple. * Kokubun Kofun Cluster - Includes {{ill|Kabutozuka Kofun|ja|甲塚古墳 (下野市)|12=draft}}, Sannōzuka Kofun, Atago Shrine Kofun, and Marutsuka Kofun. * [[:en:Shimotsuke_Provincial_Capital|Shimotsuke Provincial Capital]]

revision.text:
[[File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, chumon-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins (National Historic Site)}}]] [[File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, chumon-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins (National Historic Site)}}]] {{座標一覧}} '''Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins''' (下野国分寺跡) are the remains of an ancient [[Buddhist temple]] in [[Shimotsuke City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
It is part of the network of [[provincial temples]] established across Japan by Emperor [[Shōmu]] during the [[Nara period]]. The area has been developed as a historic site park.
This article also discusses the '''Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins''', another national historic site. For information about the current temple, see '''{{ill|Shimotsuke Kokubunji|ja|下野国分寺|12=draft}}'''.
== Overview == The temple ruins are located on a plateau in western [[Shimotsuke City]], between the [[Sugata River]] and the [[Omoi River]] (栃木県). On the opposite bank of the Omoi River, the [[Shimotsuke Kokufu Ruins]] remain. The region, home to some of the largest [[keyhole-shaped tumuli]] (前方後円墳) in Tochigi Prefecture, such as the [[Azuma Kofun]], [[:en:Biwazuka Kofun|Biwazuka Kofun]], and [[Marishitenzuka Kofun]], was a base for the [[Shimotsukeno clan]] during ancient times.
The Kokubunji and Kokubun-niji Ruins are located approximately 600 meters apart from each other, oriented east to west. They have been preserved as part of the '''Tenpyō Hill Park''' (天平の丘公園) within the broader [[Fudoki Hill]] historic site area. The park features a variety of cherry trees, including offspring of the [[Three Great Cherry Trees of Japan]] ([[Jindai Zakura]], [[Usuzumi Zakura]], and [[Miharu Takizakura]]) and [[Ishiwari Zakura]], as well as many varieties of [[Yaezakura]]. During cherry blossom season, the area attracts large crowds of flower-viewers.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20220328132917/https://www.ana.co.jp/ja/jp/japan-travel-planner/tochigi/0000017.html Tenpyō Hill Park]. ANA.</ref>
The surrounding forest, mainly consisting of [[konara]] oak, [[kunugi]] oak, and [[zelkova]], represents a prime example of lowland forests in northern [[Kantō]], designated as the Kokubunji Greenery Environmental Conservation Area by Tochigi Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.tochigi.lg.jp/d04/30jikannti/09kokubunji.html|title=09 国分寺緑地環境保全地域|website=Tochigi Prefecture|accessdate=2024-11-11}}</ref>
== Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins == The temple site measures 457 meters north-south and 413 meters east-west.<ref name=shimotsuke>[http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002400/hpg000002382.htm Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins] (Shimotsuke City Website).</ref> The temple layout follows the Tōdaiji-style with the South Gate, Middle Gate, Golden Hall, and Lecture Hall aligned in a straight line from south to north. The Middle Gate and Golden Hall are connected by a cloister.<ref name=shimotsuke/> A seven-story pagoda, estimated from the size of its base, stood east of the cloister. Other features include bell towers and sutra repositories between the Golden Hall and Lecture Hall.<ref name=shimotsuke/>
Excavation revealed five distinct phases of development:<ref name=shimotsuke/> * Phase 1: Mid-8th century - Founding period, construction of the pagoda and Golden Hall. * Phase 2: Late 8th to early 9th century - Completion tier 2]][[Category: of major buildings, construction of a wooden-pillar fence around the temple grounds. * Phase 3: Late 9th century - Renovation period, replacement of the wooden-pillar fence with an earthen wall. * Phase 4: From the 10th century - Decline, cessation of repairs to main buildings and moat re-digging. * Phase 5: By the 11th-12th centuries, the temple’s flame of faith was extinguished.
The main features of the temple are: ; Golden Hall : Foundation: 21 meters north-south, 33.6 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 13.8 meters (4 bays) north-south, 25.8 meters (7 bays) east-west. ; Lecture Hall : Foundation: 16.2 meters north-south, 24.6 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 12.6 meters (4 bays) north-south, 21 meters (7 bays) east-west. ; Cloisters ; Bell Tower : Foundation: 12 meters north-south, 9 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 9 meters (3 bays) north-south, 6 meters (2 bays) east-west. ; Sutra Repository : Foundation: 12 meters north-south, 9 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 9 meters (3 bays) north-south, 6 meters (2 bays) east-west. ; Middle Gate : Foundation: 10.2 meters north-south, 22.8 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 2 bays north-south, 5 bays east-west. ; Pagoda : Foundation: 18 meters square. Building dimensions: 3 bays square, estimated height of over 60 meters. A reconstruction model is displayed in the Fudoki Hill Museum. ; South Gate
<gallery> File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato kondou.JPG|Golden Hall Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato koudou.JPG|Lecture Hall Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato soubou.JPG|Cloister Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato shourou.JPG|Bell Tower Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato kyouzou.JPG|Sutra Repository Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato chuumon.JPG|Middle Gate Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato tou.JPG|Pagoda Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato nandaimon.JPG|South Gate Ruins </gallery>
== Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins == Located approximately 600 meters east of the Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins. The temple site measures 270 meters north-south and 145 meters east-west, with a projecting section on the east side measuring 211 meters north-south and 52 meters east-west.<ref name=shimotsukeniji>[http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002500/hpg000002418.htm Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins] (Shimotsuke City Website).</ref> The layout is similar to that of Shimotsuke Kokubunji, but no pagoda was constructed.<ref name=shimotsukeniji/>
<gallery> File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, kondou.jpg|Golden Hall Ruins File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, koudou.jpg|Lecture Hall Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunniji-ato shourou.JPG|Bell Tower Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunniji-ato kyouzou.JPG|Sutra Repository Ruins File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, chumon-1.jpg|Middle Gate Ruins </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins - Designated on March 3, 1921, with additions to the designated area on March 2, 2005, and March 10, 2015.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|463|Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins}}</ref> * Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins - Designated on April 9, 1965, with an addition on July 28, 1977.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|485|Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}}</ref>
== Location == '''Addresses''' * Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins: {{Coord|36|23|06.21|N|139|48|21.95|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins}} * Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins: {{Coord|36|23|06.98|N|139|48|44.73|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|name=Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}}
'''Related Facilities''' * Tochigi Prefectural Fudoki Hill Museum ** Admission: 100 yen for adults, 50 yen for high school and university students ** Hours: 9:00 AM–5:00 PM (last admission at 4:30 PM) ** Closed: Mondays (open on holidays and Prefectural Day), the day after holidays (open on weekends), year-end and New Year holidays
'''Access''' * Train: [[East Japan Railway Company]] ([[JR East]]) [[Utsunomiya Line]] ([[Tōhoku Main Line]]) [[Koganei Station]] - Approximately 3.5 km ** Bicycle rentals available at the station
'''Nearby''' * {{ill|Shimotsuke Kokubunji|ja|下野国分寺|12=draft}} - Successor temple. * Kokubun Kofun Cluster - Includes {{ill|Kabutozuka Kofun|ja|甲塚古墳 (下野市)|12=draft}}, Sannōzuka Kofun, Atago Shrine Kofun, and Marutsuka Kofun. * [[:en:Shimotsuke_Provincial_Capital|Shimotsuke Provincial Capital]]
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== References == * On-site explanatory boards * {{Cite book|title=Historical Place Names of Tochigi Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|chapter=Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins and Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimotsuke Kokubunji}} {{Commonscat|Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins|下野国分尼寺跡}} * [http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002400/hpg000002382.htm Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins], [http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002500/hpg000002418.htm Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins] - Shimotsuke City Website * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|463}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|485|Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}} * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/1341002.htm Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins], [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/1341016.htm Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins] - Tochigi Cultural Assets * [http://shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=5 Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins], [http://shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=7 Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins] - Shimotsuke Cultural Heritage <!--Shimotsuke Kokubunji ruins--> <!--Shimotsuke Kokubunji ruins-->

'''Related Facilities''' * Tochigi Prefectural Fudoki Hill Museum ** Admission: 100 yen for adults, 50 yen for high school and university students ** Hours: 9:00 AM–5:00 PM (last admission at 4:30 PM) ** Closed: Mondays (open on holidays and Prefectural Day), the day after holidays (open on weekends), year-end and New Year holidays
'''Access''' * Train: [[East Japan Railway Company]] ([[JR East]]) [[Utsunomiya Line]] ([[Tōhoku Main Line]]) [[Koganei Station]] - Approximately 3.5 km ** Bicycle rentals available at the station
'''Nearby''' * {{ill|Shimotsuke Kokubunji|ja|下野国分寺|12=draft}} - Successor temple. * Kokubun Kofun Cluster - Includes {{ill|Kabutozuka Kofun|ja|甲塚古墳 (下野市)|12=draft}}, Sannōzuka Kofun, Atago Shrine Kofun, and Marutsuka Kofun. * [[:en:Shimotsuke_Provincial_Capital|Shimotsuke Provincial Capital]]
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== References == * On-site explanatory boards * {{Cite book|title=Historical Place Names of Tochigi Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|chapter=Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins and Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimotsuke Kokubunji}} {{Commonscat|Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins|下野国分尼寺跡}} * [http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002400/hpg000002382.htm Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins], [http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002500/hpg000002418.htm Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins] - Shimotsuke City Website * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|463}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|485|Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}} * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/1341002.htm Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins], [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/1341016.htm Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins] - Tochigi Cultural Assets * [http://shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=5 Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins], [http://shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=7 Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins] - Shimotsuke Cultural Heritage <!--Shimotsuke Kokubunji ruins--> <!--Shimotsuke Kokubunji ruins-->

revision.comment: Text replacement - "座標一覧" to "GeoGroup"
revision.text:
[[File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, chumon-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins (National Historic Site)}}]] [[File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, chumon-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins (National Historic Site)}}]] {{GeoGroup}} '''Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins''' (下野国分寺跡) are the remains of an ancient [[Buddhist temple]] in [[Shimotsuke City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
It is part of the network of [[provincial temples]] established across Japan by Emperor [[Shōmu]] during the [[Nara period]]. The area has been developed as a historic site park.
This article also discusses the '''Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins''', another national historic site. For information about the current temple, see '''{{ill|Shimotsuke Kokubunji|ja|下野国分寺|12=draft}}'''.
== Overview == The temple ruins are located on a plateau in western [[Shimotsuke City]], between the [[Sugata River]] and the [[Omoi River]] (栃木県). On the opposite bank of the Omoi River, the [[Shimotsuke Kokufu Ruins]] remain. The region, home to some of the largest [[keyhole-shaped tumuli]] (前方後円墳) in Tochigi Prefecture, such as the [[Azuma Kofun]], [[:en:Biwazuka Kofun|Biwazuka Kofun]], and [[Marishitenzuka Kofun]], was a base for the [[Shimotsukeno clan]] during ancient times.
The Kokubunji and Kokubun-niji Ruins are located approximately 600 meters apart from each other, oriented east to west. They have been preserved as part of the '''Tenpyō Hill Park''' (天平の丘公園) within the broader [[Fudoki Hill]] historic site area. The park features a variety of cherry trees, including offspring of the [[Three Great Cherry Trees of Japan]] ([[Jindai Zakura]], [[Usuzumi Zakura]], and [[Miharu Takizakura]]) and [[Ishiwari Zakura]], as well as many varieties of [[Yaezakura]]. During cherry blossom season, the area attracts large crowds of flower-viewers.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20220328132917/https://www.ana.co.jp/ja/jp/japan-travel-planner/tochigi/0000017.html Tenpyō Hill Park]. ANA.</ref>
The surrounding forest, mainly consisting of [[konara]] oak, [[kunugi]] oak, and [[zelkova]], represents a prime example of lowland forests in northern [[Kantō]], designated as the Kokubunji Greenery Environmental Conservation Area by Tochigi Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.tochigi.lg.jp/d04/30jikannti/09kokubunji.html|title=09 国分寺緑地環境保全地域|website=Tochigi Prefecture|accessdate=2024-11-11}}</ref>
== Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins == The temple site measures 457 meters north-south and 413 meters east-west.<ref name=shimotsuke>[http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002400/hpg000002382.htm Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins] (Shimotsuke City Website).</ref> The temple layout follows the Tōdaiji-style with the South Gate, Middle Gate, Golden Hall, and Lecture Hall aligned in a straight line from south to north. The Middle Gate and Golden Hall are connected by a cloister.<ref name=shimotsuke/> A seven-story pagoda, estimated from the size of its base, stood east of the cloister. Other features include bell towers and sutra repositories between the Golden Hall and Lecture Hall.<ref name=shimotsuke/>
Excavation revealed five distinct phases of development:<ref name=shimotsuke/> * Phase 1: Mid-8th century - Founding period, construction of the pagoda and Golden Hall. * Phase 2: Late 8th to early 9th century - Completion tier 2]][[Category: of major buildings, construction of a wooden-pillar fence around the temple grounds. * Phase 3: Late 9th century - Renovation period, replacement of the wooden-pillar fence with an earthen wall. * Phase 4: From the 10th century - Decline, cessation of repairs to main buildings and moat re-digging. * Phase 5: By the 11th-12th centuries, the temple’s flame of faith was extinguished.
The main features of the temple are: ; Golden Hall : Foundation: 21 meters north-south, 33.6 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 13.8 meters (4 bays) north-south, 25.8 meters (7 bays) east-west. ; Lecture Hall : Foundation: 16.2 meters north-south, 24.6 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 12.6 meters (4 bays) north-south, 21 meters (7 bays) east-west. ; Cloisters ; Bell Tower : Foundation: 12 meters north-south, 9 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 9 meters (3 bays) north-south, 6 meters (2 bays) east-west. ; Sutra Repository : Foundation: 12 meters north-south, 9 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 9 meters (3 bays) north-south, 6 meters (2 bays) east-west. ; Middle Gate : Foundation: 10.2 meters north-south, 22.8 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 2 bays north-south, 5 bays east-west. ; Pagoda : Foundation: 18 meters square. Building dimensions: 3 bays square, estimated height of over 60 meters. A reconstruction model is displayed in the Fudoki Hill Museum. ; South Gate
<gallery> File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato kondou.JPG|Golden Hall Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato koudou.JPG|Lecture Hall Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato soubou.JPG|Cloister Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato shourou.JPG|Bell Tower Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato kyouzou.JPG|Sutra Repository Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato chuumon.JPG|Middle Gate Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato tou.JPG|Pagoda Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato nandaimon.JPG|South Gate Ruins </gallery>
== Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins == Located approximately 600 meters east of the Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins. The temple site measures 270 meters north-south and 145 meters east-west, with a projecting section on the east side measuring 211 meters north-south and 52 meters east-west.<ref name=shimotsukeniji>[http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002500/hpg000002418.htm Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins] (Shimotsuke City Website).</ref> The layout is similar to that of Shimotsuke Kokubunji, but no pagoda was constructed.<ref name=shimotsukeniji/>
<gallery> File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, kondou.jpg|Golden Hall Ruins File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, koudou.jpg|Lecture Hall Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunniji-ato shourou.JPG|Bell Tower Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunniji-ato kyouzou.JPG|Sutra Repository Ruins File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, chumon-1.jpg|Middle Gate Ruins </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins - Designated on March 3, 1921, with additions to the designated area on March 2, 2005, and March 10, 2015.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|463|Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins}}</ref> * Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins - Designated on April 9, 1965, with an addition on July 28, 1977.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|485|Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}}</ref>
== Location == '''Addresses''' * Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins: {{Coord|36|23|06.21|N|139|48|21.95|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins}} * Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins: {{Coord|36|23|06.98|N|139|48|44.73|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|name=Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}}
'''Related Facilities''' * Tochigi Prefectural Fudoki Hill Museum ** Admission: 100 yen for adults, 50 yen for high school and university students ** Hours: 9:00 AM–5:00 PM (last admission at 4:30 PM) ** Closed: Mondays (open on holidays and Prefectural Day), the day after holidays (open on weekends), year-end and New Year holidays
'''Access''' * Train: [[East Japan Railway Company]] ([[JR East]]) [[Utsunomiya Line]] ([[Tōhoku Main Line]]) [[Koganei Station]] - Approximately 3.5 km ** Bicycle rentals available at the station
'''Nearby''' * {{ill|Shimotsuke Kokubunji|ja|下野国分寺|12=draft}} - Successor temple. * Kokubun Kofun Cluster - Includes {{ill|Kabutozuka Kofun|ja|甲塚古墳 (下野市)|12=draft}}, Sannōzuka Kofun, Atago Shrine Kofun, and Marutsuka Kofun. * [[:en:Shimotsuke_Provincial_Capital|Shimotsuke Provincial Capital]]
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== References == * On-site explanatory boards * {{Cite book|title=Historical Place Names of Tochigi Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|chapter=Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins and Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimotsuke Kokubunji}} {{Commonscat|Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins|下野国分尼寺跡}} * [http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002400/hpg000002382.htm Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins], [http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002500/hpg000002418.htm Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins] - Shimotsuke City Website * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|463}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|485|Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}} * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/1341002.htm Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins], [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/1341016.htm Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins] - Tochigi Cultural Assets * [http://shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=5 Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins], [http://shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=7 Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins] - Shimotsuke Cultural Heritage <!--Shimotsuke Kokubunji ruins--> <!--Shimotsuke Kokubunji ruins-->
{{draft categories| [[Category:Archaeological sites in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Shimotsuke City|Ruins of Shimotsuke Kokubunji]] [[Category:History of Shimotsuke City]] [[Category:Provincial temples]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shimotsuke Province|Temple ruins of Shimotsuke Kokubunji]] [[Category:Ruins of Buddhist temples in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shimotsuke Kokubunji ruins]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Shimotsuke Kokubunji ruins]] [[ja:下野国分寺跡]] [[en:Shimotsuke Kokubunji ruins]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shimotsuke Kokubunji ruins}}

They have been preserved as part of the '''Tenpyō Hill Park''' (天平の丘公園) within the broader [[Fudoki Hill]] historic site area. The park features a variety of cherry trees, including offspring of the [[Three Great Cherry Trees of Japan]] ([[Jindai Zakura]], [[Usuzumi Zakura]], and [[Miharu Takizakura]]) and [[Ishiwari Zakura]], as well as many varieties of [[Yaezakura]]. During cherry blossom season, the area attracts large crowds of flower-viewers.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20220328132917/https://www.ana.co.jp/ja/jp/japan-travel-planner/tochigi/0000017.html Tenpyō Hill Park]. ANA.</ref>
The surrounding forest, mainly consisting of [[konara]] oak, [[kunugi]] oak, and [[zelkova]], represents a prime example of lowland forests in northern [[Kantō]], designated as the Kokubunji Greenery Environmental Conservation Area by Tochigi Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.tochigi.lg.jp/d04/30jikannti/09kokubunji.html|title=09 国分寺緑地環境保全地域|website=Tochigi Prefecture|accessdate=2024-11-11}}</ref>
== Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins == The temple site measures 457 meters north-south and 413 meters east-west.<ref name=shimotsuke>[http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002400/hpg000002382.htm Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins] (Shimotsuke City Website).</ref> The temple layout follows the Tōdaiji-style with the South Gate, Middle Gate, Golden Hall, and Lecture Hall aligned in a straight line from south to north. The Middle Gate and Golden Hall are connected by a cloister.<ref name=shimotsuke/> A seven-story pagoda, estimated from the size of its base, stood east of the cloister. Other features include bell towers and sutra repositories between the Golden Hall and Lecture Hall.<ref name=shimotsuke/>
Excavation revealed five distinct phases of development:<ref name=shimotsuke/> * Phase 1: Mid-8th century - Founding period, construction of the pagoda and Golden Hall. * Phase 2: Late 8th to early 9th century - Completion tier 2]][[Category: of major buildings, construction of a wooden-pillar fence around the temple grounds. * Phase 3: Late 9th century - Renovation period, replacement of the wooden-pillar fence with an earthen wall. * Phase 4: From the 10th century - Decline, cessation of repairs to main buildings and moat re-digging. * Phase 5: By the 11th-12th centuries, the temple’s flame of faith was extinguished.
The main features of the temple are: ; Golden Hall : Foundation: 21 meters north-south, 33.6 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 13.8 meters (4 bays) north-south, 25.8 meters (7 bays) east-west. ; Lecture Hall : Foundation: 16.2 meters north-south, 24.6 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 12.6 meters (4 bays) north-south, 21 meters (7 bays) east-west. ; Cloisters ; Bell Tower : Foundation: 12 meters north-south, 9 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 9 meters (3 bays) north-south, 6 meters (2 bays) east-west. ; Sutra Repository : Foundation: 12 meters north-south, 9 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 9 meters (3 bays) north-south, 6 meters (2 bays) east-west. ; Middle Gate : Foundation: 10.2 meters north-south, 22.8 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 2 bays north-south, 5 bays east-west. ; Pagoda : Foundation: 18 meters square. Building dimensions: 3 bays square, estimated height of over 60 meters. A reconstruction model is displayed in the Fudoki Hill Museum. ; South Gate
<gallery> File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato kondou.JPG|Golden Hall Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato koudou.JPG|Lecture Hall Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato soubou.JPG|Cloister Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato shourou.JPG|Bell Tower Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato kyouzou.JPG|Sutra Repository Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato chuumon.JPG|Middle Gate Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato tou.JPG|Pagoda Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato nandaimon.JPG|South Gate Ruins </gallery>
== Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins == Located approximately 600 meters east of the Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins. The temple site measures 270 meters north-south and 145 meters east-west, with a projecting section on the east side measuring 211 meters north-south and 52 meters east-west.<ref name=shimotsukeniji>[http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002500/hpg000002418.htm Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins] (Shimotsuke City Website).</ref> The layout is similar to that of Shimotsuke Kokubunji, but no pagoda was constructed.<ref name=shimotsukeniji/>
<gallery> File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, kondou.jpg|Golden Hall Ruins File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, koudou.jpg|Lecture Hall Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunniji-ato shourou.JPG|Bell Tower Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunniji-ato kyouzou.JPG|Sutra Repository Ruins File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, chumon-1.jpg|Middle Gate Ruins </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins - Designated on March 3, 1921, with additions to the designated area on March 2, 2005, and March 10, 2015.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|463|Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins}}</ref> * Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins - Designated on April 9, 1965, with an addition on July 28, 1977.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|485|Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}}</ref>
== Location == '''Addresses''' * Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins: {{Coord|36|23|06.21|N|139|48|21.95|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins}} * Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins: {{Coord|36|23|06.98|N|139|48|44.73|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|name=Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}}
'''Related Facilities''' * Tochigi Prefectural Fudoki Hill Museum ** Admission: 100 yen for adults, 50 yen for high school and university students ** Hours: 9:00 AM–5:00 PM (last admission at 4:30 PM) ** Closed: Mondays (open on holidays and Prefectural Day), the day after holidays (open on weekends), year-end and New Year holidays
'''Access''' * Train: [[East Japan Railway Company]] ([[JR East]]) [[Utsunomiya Line]] ([[Tōhoku Main Line]]) [[Koganei Station]] - Approximately 3.5 km ** Bicycle rentals available at the station
'''Nearby''' * {{ill|Shimotsuke Kokubunji|ja|下野国分寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Successor temple. * Kokubun Kofun Cluster - Includes {{ill|Kabutozuka Kofun|ja|甲塚古墳 (下野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Sannōzuka Kofun, Atago Shrine Kofun, and Marutsuka Kofun. * [[:en:Shimotsuke_Provincial_Capital|Shimotsuke Provincial Capital]]
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== References == * On-site explanatory boards * {{Cite book|title=Historical Place Names of Tochigi Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|chapter=Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins and Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimotsuke Kokubunji}} {{Commonscat|Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins|下野国分尼寺跡}} * [http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002400/hpg000002382.htm Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins], [http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002500/hpg000002418.htm Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins] - Shimotsuke City Website * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|463}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|485|Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}} * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/1341002.htm Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins], [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/1341016.htm Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins] - Tochigi Cultural Assets * [http://shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=5 Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins], [http://shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=7 Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins] - Shimotsuke Cultural Heritage
{{draft categories|
}}
{{draft categories| [[Category:Archaeological sites in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Shimotsuke City|Ruins of Shimotsuke Kokubunji]] [[Category:History of Shimotsuke City]] [[Category:Provincial temples]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shimotsuke Province|Temple ruins of Shimotsuke Kokubunji]] [[Category:Ruins of Buddhist temples in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shimotsuke Kokubunji ruins]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }}

revision.comment: 32 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:下野国分寺跡 up to rev 102535295
revision.text:
[[File:下野国分寺跡 伽藍.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|下野国分寺跡（国の史跡）}}]] [[File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, chumon-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|下野国分尼寺跡（国の史跡）}}]] {{座標一覧}} '''下野国分寺跡'''（しもつけこくぶんじあと）は、[[栃木県]][[下野市]]にある古代[[寺院]]跡。国の[[史跡]]に指定されている。 [[奈良時代]]に[[聖武天皇]]の詔により日本各地に建立された[[国分寺]]のうち、[[下野国]]国分寺の寺院跡にあたる。現在は整備され、史跡公園となっている。 本項では同じく国の史跡である'''下野国分尼寺跡'''についても解説する。現寺院については'''[[下野国分寺]]'''を参照。 == 概要 == [[下野市]]西部、[[姿川]]と[[思川 (栃木県)|思川]]に挟まれた台地上に残る寺院跡である。思川の対岸には[[下野国庁跡]]も残っている。一帯は栃木県内最大級の[[前方後円墳]]である[[吾妻古墳]]・[[琵琶塚古墳]]・[[摩利支天塚古墳]]が残るように、古代から[[下毛野氏]]の本拠地として先進地帯であった。 国分寺・国分尼寺跡は600メートルほどの距離で東西に並んで発掘されており、現在は下野市'''天平の丘公園'''として整備されている（[[風土記の丘]]）。公園には日本三大桜（[[神代桜]]・[[淡墨桜]]・[[三春滝桜]]）と[[石割桜]]の子孫樹や各種[[ヤエザクラ]]などの多品種の[[サクラ]]が植栽されており、春の開花時期は大勢の[[花見]]客でにぎわう[https://web.archive.org/web/20220328132917/https://www.ana.co.jp/ja/jp/japan-travel-planner/tochigi/0000017.html 天平の丘公園.] ANA。 また、史跡周辺は[[コナラ]]や[[クヌギ]]、[[ケヤキ]]などを主とする北関東を代表する平地林となっており、栃木県により国分寺緑地環境保全地域に指定されている{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.tochigi.lg.jp/d04/30jikannti/09kokubunji.html|title=09 国分寺（こくぶんじ）緑地環境保全地域|website=栃木県 |accessdate=2024-11-11}}。 == 下野国分寺跡 == 寺域は南北457メートル・東西413メートル[http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002400/hpg000002382.htm 下野国分寺跡]（下野市ホームページ）。。伽藍は奈良[[東大寺]]同様の東大寺式伽藍配置で、南大門・中門・金堂・講堂が南北一直線上に並び、中門と金堂は回廊で接続している。塔は回廊の外側東方に設けられ、その[[基壇]]の大きさから七重塔であったと推定されている。そのほか、金堂・講堂の間の東西には鐘楼、経蔵が設けられていた。 発掘調査の結果、1期から5期に区分されている。 * 1期：8世紀中葉 - 創建期。塔・金堂などが造営される。 * 2期：8世紀後半から9世紀前半代 - 主要堂塔が完成。伽藍地は掘立柱塀で囲まれる。 * 3期：9世紀後半代 - 改修期。伽藍地は縮小され掘立柱塀は築地塀に建て替えられる。 * 4期：10世紀以降 - 衰退期。主要堂塔の補修・溝の掘り直しが行われなくなる。 終焉は不明ながら、発掘の結果からは11世紀-12世紀代まで法灯が伝承されたと見られている。 各伽藍の詳細は次の通り。 ; 金堂 : 基壇は南北21メートル、東西33.6メートル。建物は南北13.8メートル（4間）、東西25.8メートル（7間）。 ; 講堂 : 基壇は南北16.2メートル、東西24.6メートル。建物は南北12.6メートル（4間）、東西21メートル（7間）。 ; 僧房 ; 鐘楼 : 基壇は南北12メートル、東西9メートル。建物は南北9メートル（3間）、東西6メートル（2間）。 ; 経蔵 : 基壇は南北12メートル、東西9メートル。建物は南北9メートル（3間）、東西6メートル（2間）。 ; 中門 : 基壇は南北10.2メートル、東西22.8メートル。建物は南北2間、東西5間。 ; 塔 : 基壇は南北・東西18メートル。建物は南北・東西3間。高さ60メートルを超える七重塔と推定。風土記の丘資料館内に復元予想模型が展示されている。 ; 南大門 File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato kondou.JPG|金堂跡 File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato koudou.JPG|講堂跡 File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato soubou.JPG|僧房跡 File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato shourou.JPG|鐘楼跡 File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato kyouzou.JPG|経蔵跡 File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato chuumon.JPG|中門跡 File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato tou.JPG|塔跡 File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato nandaimon.JPG|南大門跡 ファイル:下野国分寺跡出土 鬼面文鬼瓦 (J-35013).JPG|鬼面文鬼瓦
{{small|[[東京国立博物館]]展示。}} ファイル:下野国分寺跡出土 軒瓦.JPG|軒瓦
{{small|東京国立博物館展示。}} == 下野国分尼寺跡 == 下野国分寺跡の東方約600メートルに位置する。寺域は南北約270メートル・東西約145メートルで、その東側に南北約211メートル・東西約52メートルの張り出し部分が存在する[http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002500/hpg000002418.htm 下野国分尼寺跡]（下野市ホームページ）。。伽藍配置は国分寺同様に東大寺式であるが、塔は建てられなかった。 File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, kondou.jpg|金堂跡 File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, koudou.jpg|講堂跡 File:Shimotsuke-kokubunniji-ato shourou.JPG|鐘楼跡 File:Shimotsuke-kokubunniji-ato kyouzou.JPG|経蔵跡 File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, chumon-1.jpg|中門跡 == 文化財 == === 国の史跡 === * 下野国分寺跡 - 大正10年3月3日指定、平成17年3月2日に史跡範囲を追加指定、平成27年3月10日に史跡範囲を追加指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|463|下野国分寺跡}}。 * 下野国分尼寺跡 - 昭和40年4月9日指定、昭和52年7月28日に史跡範囲を追加指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|485|下野国分尼寺跡}}。 == 現地情報 == '''所在地''' * 国分寺跡：[[栃木県]][[下野市]]国分寺（{{Coord|36|23|06.21|N|139|48|21.95|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=下野国分寺跡}}） * 国分尼寺跡：栃木県下野市国分寺（{{Coord|36|23|06.98|N|139|48|44.73|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|name=下野国分尼寺跡}}） '''関連施設''' * 栃木県立しもつけ風土記の丘資料館 ** 入館料：一般100円、高校生・大学生50円 ** 開館時間：午前9時-午後5時（入館は午後4時30分まで） ** 休館日：月曜日（祝日・県民の日は開館）、祝日の翌日（土・日は開館）、年末年始 '''交通アクセス''' * 鉄道：[[東日本旅客鉄道]]（JR東日本）[[宇都宮線]]（[[東北本線]]） [[小金井駅]] - 約3.5km ** 駅前にレンタサイクルあり '''周辺''' [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke) zenkei.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[甲塚古墳 (下野市)|甲塚古墳]]}}{{small|国分寺跡に隣接して残る古墳。}}]] * [[下野国分寺]]（{{Coord|36|23|26.56|N|139|48|53.48|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|name=国分寺（現寺院）}}） - 後継寺院。 * 国分古墳群 - [[甲塚古墳 (下野市)|甲塚古墳]]、山王塚古墳、愛宕神社古墳、丸塚古墳など。 * [[下野国庁跡]]（{{Coord|36|22|47.71|N|139|47|06.64|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|name=下野国庁跡）}}） == 脚注 == {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 公園内説明板 * 『[[日本歴史地名大系]] 栃木県の地名』（[[平凡社]]）下都賀郡国分寺町 下野国分寺跡・下野国分尼寺跡項 == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Shimotsuke Kokubunji}} {{Commonscat|Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji|下野国分尼寺跡}} * [http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002400/hpg000002382.htm 下野国分寺跡]、[http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002500/hpg000002418.htm 下野国分尼寺跡] - 下野市ホームページ * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|463}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|485|下野国分尼寺跡}} * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/1341002.htm 下野国分寺跡]、[http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/1341016.htm 下野国分尼寺跡] - とちぎの文化財 * [http://shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=5 下野国分寺跡]、[http://shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=7 下野国分尼寺跡] - しもつけ市の文化財 {{国分寺}} {{Buddhism2}} {{リダイレクトの所属カテゴリ |redirect1=下野国分尼寺跡 |1-1=栃木県の廃寺 |1-2=栃木県の考古遺跡 |1-3=下野市の歴史 |1-4=国分尼寺 |1-5=栃木県にある国指定の史跡 |1-6=下野国 }} {{DEFAULTSORT:しもつけこくふんしあと}} [[Category:栃木県の考古遺跡]] [[Category:下野市の建築物|廃しもつけこくふんし]] [[Category:下野市の歴史]] [[Category:国分寺]] [[Category:栃木県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:下野国|寺しもつけこくふんしあと]] [[Category:栃木県の廃寺]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[下野国分寺跡]] to [[Shimotsuke Kokubunji ruins]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
[[File:下野国分寺跡 伽藍.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|下野国分寺跡（国の史跡）}}]] [[File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, chumon-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|下野国分尼寺跡（国の史跡）}}]] {{座標一覧}} '''下野国分寺跡'''（しもつけこくぶんじあと）は、[[栃木県]][[下野市]]にある古代[[寺院]]跡。国の[[史跡]]に指定されている。 [[奈良時代]]に[[聖武天皇]]の詔により日本各地に建立された[[国分寺]]のうち、[[下野国]]国分寺の寺院跡にあたる。現在は整備され、史跡公園となっている。 本項では同じく国の史跡である'''下野国分尼寺跡'''についても解説する。現寺院については'''[[下野国分寺]]'''を参照。 == 概要 == [[下野市]]西部、[[姿川]]と[[思川 (栃木県)|思川]]に挟まれた台地上に残る寺院跡である。思川の対岸には[[下野国庁跡]]も残っている。一帯は栃木県内最大級の[[前方後円墳]]である[[吾妻古墳]]・[[琵琶塚古墳]]・[[摩利支天塚古墳]]が残るように、古代から[[下毛野氏]]の本拠地として先進地帯であった。 国分寺・国分尼寺跡は600メートルほどの距離で東西に並んで発掘されており、現在は下野市'''天平の丘公園'''として整備されている（[[風土記の丘]]）。公園には日本三大桜（[[神代桜]]・[[淡墨桜]]・[[三春滝桜]]）と[[石割桜]]の子孫樹や各種[[ヤエザクラ]]などの多品種の[[サクラ]]が植栽されており、春の開花時期は大勢の[[花見]]客でにぎわう[https://web.archive.org/web/20220328132917/https://www.ana.co.jp/ja/jp/japan-travel-planner/tochigi/0000017.html 天平の丘公園.] ANA。 また、史跡周辺は[[コナラ]]や[[クヌギ]]、[[ケヤキ]]などを主とする北関東を代表する平地林となっており、栃木県により国分寺緑地環境保全地域に指定されている{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.tochigi.lg.jp/d04/30jikannti/09kokubunji.html|title=09 国分寺（こくぶんじ）緑地環境保全地域|website=栃木県 |accessdate=2024-11-11}}。 == 下野国分寺跡 == 寺域は南北457メートル・東西413メートル[http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002400/hpg000002382.htm 下野国分寺跡]（下野市ホームページ）。。伽藍は奈良[[東大寺]]同様の東大寺式伽藍配置で、南大門・中門・金堂・講堂が南北一直線上に並び、中門と金堂は回廊で接続している。塔は回廊の外側東方に設けられ、その[[基壇]]の大きさから七重塔であったと推定されている。そのほか、金堂・講堂の間の東西には鐘楼、経蔵が設けられていた。 発掘調査の結果、1期から5期に区分されている。 * 1期：8世紀中葉 - 創建期。塔・金堂などが造営される。 * 2期：8世紀後半から9世紀前半代 - 主要堂塔が完成。伽藍地は掘立柱塀で囲まれる。 * 3期：9世紀後半代 - 改修期。伽藍地は縮小され掘立柱塀は築地塀に建て替えられる。 * 4期：10世紀以降 - 衰退期。主要堂塔の補修・溝の掘り直しが行われなくなる。 終焉は不明ながら、発掘の結果からは11世紀-12世紀代まで法灯が伝承されたと見られている。 各伽藍の詳細は次の通り。 ; 金堂 : 基壇は南北21メートル、東西33.6メートル。建物は南北13.8メートル（4間）、東西25.8メートル（7間）。 ; 講堂 : 基壇は南北16.2メートル、東西24.6メートル。建物は南北12.6メートル（4間）、東西21メートル（7間）。 ; 僧房 ; 鐘楼 : 基壇は南北12メートル、東西9メートル。建物は南北9メートル（3間）、東西6メートル（2間）。 ; 経蔵 : 基壇は南北12メートル、東西9メートル。建物は南北9メートル（3間）、東西6メートル（2間）。 ; 中門 : 基壇は南北10.2メートル、東西22.8メートル。建物は南北2間、東西5間。 ; 塔 : 基壇は南北・東西18メートル。建物は南北・東西3間。高さ60メートルを超える七重塔と推定。風土記の丘資料館内に復元予想模型が展示されている。 ; 南大門 File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato kondou.JPG|金堂跡 File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato koudou.JPG|講堂跡 File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato soubou.JPG|僧房跡 File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato shourou.JPG|鐘楼跡 File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato kyouzou.JPG|経蔵跡 File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato chuumon.JPG|中門跡 File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato tou.JPG|塔跡 File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato nandaimon.JPG|南大門跡 ファイル:下野国分寺跡出土 鬼面文鬼瓦 (J-35013).JPG|鬼面文鬼瓦
{{small|[[東京国立博物館]]展示。}} ファイル:下野国分寺跡出土 軒瓦.JPG|軒瓦
{{small|東京国立博物館展示。}} == 下野国分尼寺跡 == 下野国分寺跡の東方約600メートルに位置する。寺域は南北約270メートル・東西約145メートルで、その東側に南北約211メートル・東西約52メートルの張り出し部分が存在する[http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002500/hpg000002418.htm 下野国分尼寺跡]（下野市ホームページ）。。伽藍配置は国分寺同様に東大寺式であるが、塔は建てられなかった。 File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, kondou.jpg|金堂跡 File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, koudou.jpg|講堂跡 File:Shimotsuke-kokubunniji-ato shourou.JPG|鐘楼跡 File:Shimotsuke-kokubunniji-ato kyouzou.JPG|経蔵跡 File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, chumon-1.jpg|中門跡 == 文化財 == === 国の史跡 === * 下野国分寺跡 - 大正10年3月3日指定、平成17年3月2日に史跡範囲を追加指定、平成27年3月10日に史跡範囲を追加指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|463|下野国分寺跡}}。 * 下野国分尼寺跡 - 昭和40年4月9日指定、昭和52年7月28日に史跡範囲を追加指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|485|下野国分尼寺跡}}。 == 現地情報 == '''所在地''' * 国分寺跡：[[栃木県]][[下野市]]国分寺（{{Coord|36|23|06.21|N|139|48|21.95|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=下野国分寺跡}}） * 国分尼寺跡：栃木県下野市国分寺（{{Coord|36|23|06.98|N|139|48|44.73|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|name=下野国分尼寺跡}}） '''関連施設''' * 栃木県立しもつけ風土記の丘資料館 ** 入館料：一般100円、高校生・大学生50円 ** 開館時間：午前9時-午後5時（入館は午後4時30分まで） ** 休館日：月曜日（祝日・県民の日は開館）、祝日の翌日（土・日は開館）、年末年始 '''交通アクセス''' * 鉄道：[[東日本旅客鉄道]]（JR東日本）[[宇都宮線]]（[[東北本線]]） [[小金井駅]] - 約3.5km ** 駅前にレンタサイクルあり '''周辺''' [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke) zenkei.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[甲塚古墳 (下野市)|甲塚古墳]]}}{{small|国分寺跡に隣接して残る古墳。}}]] * [[下野国分寺]]（{{Coord|36|23|26.56|N|139|48|53.48|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|name=国分寺（現寺院）}}） - 後継寺院。 * 国分古墳群 - [[甲塚古墳 (下野市)|甲塚古墳]]、山王塚古墳、愛宕神社古墳、丸塚古墳など。 * [[下野国庁跡]]（{{Coord|36|22|47.71|N|139|47|06.64|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|name=下野国庁跡）}}） == 脚注 == {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 公園内説明板 * 『[[日本歴史地名大系]] 栃木県の地名』（[[平凡社]]）下都賀郡国分寺町 下野国分寺跡・下野国分尼寺跡項 == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Shimotsuke Kokubunji}} {{Commonscat|Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji|下野国分尼寺跡}} * [http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002400/hpg000002382.htm 下野国分寺跡]、[http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002500/hpg000002418.htm 下野国分尼寺跡] - 下野市ホームページ * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|463}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|485|下野国分尼寺跡}} * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/1341002.htm 下野国分寺跡]、[http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/1341016.htm 下野国分尼寺跡] - とちぎの文化財 * [http://shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=5 下野国分寺跡]、[http://shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=7 下野国分尼寺跡] - しもつけ市の文化財 {{国分寺}} {{Buddhism2}} {{リダイレクトの所属カテゴリ |redirect1=下野国分尼寺跡 |1-1=栃木県の廃寺 |1-2=栃木県の考古遺跡 |1-3=下野市の歴史 |1-4=国分尼寺 |1-5=栃木県にある国指定の史跡 |1-6=下野国 }} {{DEFAULTSORT:しもつけこくふんしあと}} [[Category:栃木県の考古遺跡]] [[Category:下野市の建築物|廃しもつけこくふんし]] [[Category:下野市の歴史]] [[Category:国分寺]] [[Category:栃木県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:下野国|寺しもつけこくふんしあと]] [[Category:栃木県の廃寺]]

revision.text:
[[File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, chumon-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins (National Historic Site)}}]] [[File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, chumon-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins (National Historic Site)}}]] {{GeoGroup}} '''Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins''' (下野国分寺跡) are the remains of an ancient [[Buddhist temple]] in [[Shimotsuke City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
It is part of the network of [[provincial temples]] established across Japan by Emperor [[Shōmu]] during the [[Nara period]]. The area has been developed as a historic site park.
This article also discusses the '''Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins''', another national historic site. For information about the current temple, see '''{{ill|Shimotsuke Kokubunji|ja|下野国分寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'''.
== Overview == The temple ruins are located on a plateau in western [[Shimotsuke City]], between the [[Sugata River]] and the [[Omoi River]] (栃木県). On the opposite bank of the Omoi River, the [[Shimotsuke Kokufu Ruins]] remain. The region, home to some of the largest [[keyhole-shaped tumuli]] (前方後円墳) in Tochigi Prefecture, such as the [[Azuma Kofun]], [[:en:Biwazuka Kofun|Biwazuka Kofun]], and [[Marishitenzuka Kofun]], was a base for the [[Shimotsukeno clan]] during ancient times.
The Kokubunji and Kokubun-niji Ruins are located approximately 600 meters apart from each other, oriented east to west. They have been preserved as part of the '''Tenpyō Hill Park''' (天平の丘公園) within the broader [[Fudoki Hill]] historic site area. The park features a variety of cherry trees, including offspring of the [[Three Great Cherry Trees of Japan]] ([[Jindai Zakura]], [[Usuzumi Zakura]], and [[Miharu Takizakura]]) and [[Ishiwari Zakura]], as well as many varieties of [[Yaezakura]]. During cherry blossom season, the area attracts large crowds of flower-viewers.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20220328132917/https://www.ana.co.jp/ja/jp/japan-travel-planner/tochigi/0000017.html Tenpyō Hill Park]. ANA.</ref>
The surrounding forest, mainly consisting of [[konara]] oak, [[kunugi]] oak, and [[zelkova]], represents a prime example of lowland forests in northern [[Kantō]], designated as the Kokubunji Greenery Environmental Conservation Area by Tochigi Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.tochigi.lg.jp/d04/30jikannti/09kokubunji.html|title=09 国分寺緑地環境保全地域|website=Tochigi Prefecture|accessdate=2024-11-11}}</ref>
== Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins == The temple site measures 457 meters north-south and 413 meters east-west.<ref name=shimotsuke>[http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002400/hpg000002382.htm Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins] (Shimotsuke City Website).</ref> The temple layout follows the Tōdaiji-style with the South Gate, Middle Gate, Golden Hall, and Lecture Hall aligned in a straight line from south to north. The Middle Gate and Golden Hall are connected by a cloister.<ref name=shimotsuke/> A seven-story pagoda, estimated from the size of its base, stood east of the cloister. Other features include bell towers and sutra repositories between the Golden Hall and Lecture Hall.<ref name=shimotsuke/>
Excavation revealed five distinct phases of development:<ref name=shimotsuke/> * Phase 1: Mid-8th century - Founding period, construction of the pagoda and Golden Hall. * Phase 2: Late 8th to early 9th century - Completion tier 2]][[Category: of major buildings, construction of a wooden-pillar fence around the temple grounds. * Phase 3: Late 9th century - Renovation period, replacement of the wooden-pillar fence with an earthen wall. * Phase 4: From the 10th century - Decline, cessation of repairs to main buildings and moat re-digging. * Phase 5: By the 11th-12th centuries, the temple’s flame of faith was extinguished.
The main features of the temple are: ; Golden Hall : Foundation: 21 meters north-south, 33.6 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 13.8 meters (4 bays) north-south, 25.8 meters (7 bays) east-west. ; Lecture Hall : Foundation: 16.2 meters north-south, 24.6 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 12.6 meters (4 bays) north-south, 21 meters (7 bays) east-west. ; Cloisters ; Bell Tower : Foundation: 12 meters north-south, 9 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 9 meters (3 bays) north-south, 6 meters (2 bays) east-west. ; Sutra Repository : Foundation: 12 meters north-south, 9 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 9 meters (3 bays) north-south, 6 meters (2 bays) east-west. ; Middle Gate : Foundation: 10.2 meters north-south, 22.8 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 2 bays north-south, 5 bays east-west. ; Pagoda : Foundation: 18 meters square. Building dimensions: 3 bays square, estimated height of over 60 meters. A reconstruction model is displayed in the Fudoki Hill Museum. ; South Gate
<gallery> File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato kondou.JPG|Golden Hall Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato koudou.JPG|Lecture Hall Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato soubou.JPG|Cloister Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato shourou.JPG|Bell Tower Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato kyouzou.JPG|Sutra Repository Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato chuumon.JPG|Middle Gate Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato tou.JPG|Pagoda Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato nandaimon.JPG|South Gate Ruins </gallery>
== Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins == Located approximately 600 meters east of the Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins. The temple site measures 270 meters north-south and 145 meters east-west, with a projecting section on the east side measuring 211 meters north-south and 52 meters east-west.<ref name=shimotsukeniji>[http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002500/hpg000002418.htm Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins] (Shimotsuke City Website).</ref> The layout is similar to that of Shimotsuke Kokubunji, but no pagoda was constructed.<ref name=shimotsukeniji/>
<gallery> File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, kondou.jpg|Golden Hall Ruins File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, koudou.jpg|Lecture Hall Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunniji-ato shourou.JPG|Bell Tower Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunniji-ato kyouzou.JPG|Sutra Repository Ruins File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, chumon-1.jpg|Middle Gate Ruins </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins - Designated on March 3, 1921, with additions to the designated area on March 2, 2005, and March 10, 2015.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|463|Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins}}</ref> * Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins - Designated on April 9, 1965, with an addition on July 28, 1977.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|485|Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}}</ref>
== Location == '''Addresses''' * Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins: {{Coord|36|23|06.21|N|139|48|21.95|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins}} * Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins: {{Coord|36|23|06.98|N|139|48|44.73|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|name=Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}}
'''Related Facilities''' * Tochigi Prefectural Fudoki Hill Museum ** Admission: 100 yen for adults, 50 yen for high school and university students ** Hours: 9:00 AM–5:00 PM (last admission at 4:30 PM) ** Closed: Mondays (open on holidays and Prefectural Day), the day after holidays (open on weekends), year-end and New Year holidays
'''Access''' * Train: [[East Japan Railway Company]] ([[JR East]]) [[Utsunomiya Line]] ([[Tōhoku Main Line]]) [[Koganei Station]] - Approximately 3.5 km ** Bicycle rentals available at the station
'''Nearby''' * {{ill|Shimotsuke Kokubunji|ja|下野国分寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Successor temple. * Kokubun Kofun Cluster - Includes {{ill|Kabutozuka Kofun|ja|甲塚古墳 (下野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Sannōzuka Kofun, Atago Shrine Kofun, and Marutsuka Kofun. * [[:en:Shimotsuke_Provincial_Capital|Shimotsuke Provincial Capital]]
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== References == * On-site explanatory boards * {{Cite book|title=Historical Place Names of Tochigi Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|chapter=Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins and Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimotsuke Kokubunji}} {{Commonscat|Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins|下野国分尼寺跡}} * [http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002400/hpg000002382.htm Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins], [http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002500/hpg000002418.htm Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins] - Shimotsuke City Website * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|463}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|485|Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}} * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/1341002.htm Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins], [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/1341016.htm Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins] - Tochigi Cultural Assets * [http://shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=5 Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins], [http://shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=7 Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins] - Shimotsuke Cultural Heritage
{{DEFAULTSORT:Shimotsuke Kokubunji ruins}}

revision.text:
[[File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, chumon-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins (National Historic Site)}}]] [[File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, chumon-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins (National Historic Site)}}]] {{GeoGroup}} '''Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins''' (下野国分寺跡) are the remains of an ancient [[Buddhist temple]] in [[Shimotsuke City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
It is part of the network of [[provincial temples]] established across Japan by Emperor [[Shōmu]] during the [[Nara period]]. The area has been developed as a historic site park.
This article also discusses the '''Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins''', another national historic site. For information about the current temple, see '''{{ill|Shimotsuke Kokubunji|ja|下野国分寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'''.

The Kokubunji and Kokubun-niji Ruins are located approximately 600 meters apart from each other, oriented east to west. They have been preserved as part of the '''Tenpyō Hill Park''' (天平の丘公園) within the broader [[Fudoki Hill]] historic site area. The park features a variety of cherry trees, including offspring of the [[Three Great Cherry Trees of Japan]] ([[Jindai Zakura]], [[Usuzumi Zakura]], and [[Miharu Takizakura]]) and [[Ishiwari Zakura]], as well as many varieties of [[Yaezakura]]. During cherry blossom season, the area attracts large crowds of flower-viewers.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20220328132917/https://www.ana.co.jp/ja/jp/japan-travel-planner/tochigi/0000017.html Tenpyō Hill Park]. ANA.</ref>
The surrounding forest, mainly consisting of [[konara]] oak, [[kunugi]] oak, and [[zelkova]], represents a prime example of lowland forests in northern [[Kantō]], designated as the Kokubunji Greenery Environmental Conservation Area by Tochigi Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.tochigi.lg.jp/d04/30jikannti/09kokubunji.html|title=09 国分寺緑地環境保全地域|website=Tochigi Prefecture|accessdate=2024-11-11}}</ref>
== Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins == The temple site measures 457 meters north-south and 413 meters east-west.<ref name=shimotsuke>[http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002400/hpg000002382.htm Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins] (Shimotsuke City Website).</ref> The temple layout follows the Tōdaiji-style with the South Gate, Middle Gate, Golden Hall, and Lecture Hall aligned in a straight line from south to north. The Middle Gate and Golden Hall are connected by a cloister.<ref name=shimotsuke/> A seven-story pagoda, estimated from the size of its base, stood east of the cloister. Other features include bell towers and sutra repositories between the Golden Hall and Lecture Hall.<ref name=shimotsuke/>
Excavation revealed five distinct phases of development:<ref name=shimotsuke/> * Phase 1: Mid-8th century - Founding period, construction of the pagoda and Golden Hall. * Phase 2: Late 8th to early 9th century - Completion tier 2]][[Category: of major buildings, construction of a wooden-pillar fence around the temple grounds. * Phase 3: Late 9th century - Renovation period, replacement of the wooden-pillar fence with an earthen wall. * Phase 4: From the 10th century - Decline, cessation of repairs to main buildings and moat re-digging. * Phase 5: By the 11th-12th centuries, the temple’s flame of faith was extinguished.
The main features of the temple are: ; Golden Hall : Foundation: 21 meters north-south, 33.6 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 13.8 meters (4 bays) north-south, 25.8 meters (7 bays) east-west. ; Lecture Hall : Foundation: 16.2 meters north-south, 24.6 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 12.6 meters (4 bays) north-south, 21 meters (7 bays) east-west. ; Cloisters ; Bell Tower : Foundation: 12 meters north-south, 9 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 9 meters (3 bays) north-south, 6 meters (2 bays) east-west. ; Sutra Repository : Foundation: 12 meters north-south, 9 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 9 meters (3 bays) north-south, 6 meters (2 bays) east-west. ; Middle Gate : Foundation: 10.2 meters north-south, 22.8 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 2 bays north-south, 5 bays east-west. ; Pagoda : Foundation: 18 meters square. Building dimensions: 3 bays square, estimated height of over 60 meters. A reconstruction model is displayed in the Fudoki Hill Museum. ; South Gate
<gallery> File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato kondou.JPG|Golden Hall Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato koudou.JPG|Lecture Hall Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato soubou.JPG|Cloister Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato shourou.JPG|Bell Tower Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato kyouzou.JPG|Sutra Repository Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato chuumon.JPG|Middle Gate Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato tou.JPG|Pagoda Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato nandaimon.JPG|South Gate Ruins </gallery>
== Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins == Located approximately 600 meters east of the Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins. The temple site measures 270 meters north-south and 145 meters east-west, with a projecting section on the east side measuring 211 meters north-south and 52 meters east-west.<ref name=shimotsukeniji>[http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002500/hpg000002418.htm Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins] (Shimotsuke City Website).</ref> The layout is similar to that of Shimotsuke Kokubunji, but no pagoda was constructed.<ref name=shimotsukeniji/>
<gallery> File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, kondou.jpg|Golden Hall Ruins File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, koudou.jpg|Lecture Hall Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunniji-ato shourou.JPG|Bell Tower Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunniji-ato kyouzou.JPG|Sutra Repository Ruins File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, chumon-1.jpg|Middle Gate Ruins </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins - Designated on March 3, 1921, with additions to the designated area on March 2, 2005, and March 10, 2015.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|463|Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins}}</ref> * Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins - Designated on April 9, 1965, with an addition on July 28, 1977.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|485|Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}}</ref>
== Location == '''Addresses''' * Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins: {{Coord|36|23|06.21|N|139|48|21.95|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins}} * Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins: {{Coord|36|23|06.98|N|139|48|44.73|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|name=Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}}
'''Related Facilities''' * Tochigi Prefectural Fudoki Hill Museum ** Admission: 100 yen for adults, 50 yen for high school and university students ** Hours: 9:00 AM–5:00 PM (last admission at 4:30 PM) ** Closed: Mondays (open on holidays and Prefectural Day), the day after holidays (open on weekends), year-end and New Year holidays
'''Access''' * Train: [[East Japan Railway Company]] ([[JR East]]) [[Utsunomiya Line]] ([[Tōhoku Main Line]]) [[Koganei Station]] - Approximately 3.5 km ** Bicycle rentals available at the station
'''Nearby''' * {{ill|Shimotsuke Kokubunji|ja|下野国分寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Successor temple. * Kokubun Kofun Cluster - Includes {{ill|Kabutozuka Kofun|ja|甲塚古墳 (下野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Sannōzuka Kofun, Atago Shrine Kofun, and Marutsuka Kofun. * [[:en:Shimotsuke_Provincial_Capital|Shimotsuke Provincial Capital]]
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== References == * On-site explanatory boards * {{Cite book|title=Historical Place Names of Tochigi Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|chapter=Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins and Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimotsuke Kokubunji}} {{Commonscat|Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins|下野国分尼寺跡}} * [http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002400/hpg000002382.htm Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins], [http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002500/hpg000002418.htm Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins] - Shimotsuke City Website * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|463}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|485|Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}} * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/1341002.htm Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins], [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/1341016.htm Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins] - Tochigi Cultural Assets * [http://shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=5 Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins], [http://shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=7 Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins] - Shimotsuke Cultural Heritage

revision.text:
[[File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, chumon-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins (National Historic Site)}}]] [[File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, chumon-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins (National Historic Site)}}]] {{GeoGroup}} '''Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins''' (下野国分寺跡) are the remains of an ancient [[Buddhist temple]] in [[Shimotsuke City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
It is part of the network of [[provincial temples]] established across Japan by Emperor [[Shōmu]] during the [[Nara period]]. The area has been developed as a historic site park.
This article also discusses the '''Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins''', another national historic site. For information about the current temple, see '''{{ill|Shimotsuke Kokubunji|ja|下野国分寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'''.
== Overview == The temple ruins are located on a plateau in western [[Shimotsuke City]], between the [[Sugata River]] and the [[Omoi River]] (栃木県). On the opposite bank of the Omoi River, the [[Shimotsuke Kokufu Ruins]] remain. The region, home to some of the largest [[keyhole-shaped tumuli]] (前方後円墳) in Tochigi Prefecture, such as the [[Azuma Kofun]], [[:en:Biwazuka Kofun|Biwazuka Kofun]], and [[Marishitenzuka Kofun]], was a base for the [[Shimotsukeno clan]] during ancient times.
The Kokubunji and Kokubun-niji Ruins are located approximately 600 meters apart from each other, oriented east to west. They have been preserved as part of the '''Tenpyō Hill Park''' (天平の丘公園) within the broader [[Fudoki Hill]] historic site area. The park features a variety of cherry trees, including offspring of the [[Three Great Cherry Trees of Japan]] ([[Jindai Zakura]], [[Usuzumi Zakura]], and [[Miharu Takizakura]]) and [[Ishiwari Zakura]], as well as many varieties of [[Yaezakura]]. During cherry blossom season, the area attracts large crowds of flower-viewers.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20220328132917/https://www.ana.co.jp/ja/jp/japan-travel-planner/tochigi/0000017.html Tenpyō Hill Park]. ANA.</ref>
The surrounding forest, mainly consisting of [[konara]] oak, [[kunugi]] oak, and [[zelkova]], represents a prime example of lowland forests in northern [[Kantō]], designated as the Kokubunji Greenery Environmental Conservation Area by Tochigi Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.tochigi.lg.jp/d04/30jikannti/09kokubunji.html|title=09 国分寺緑地環境保全地域|website=Tochigi Prefecture|accessdate=2024-11-11}}</ref>
== Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins == The temple site measures 457 meters north-south and 413 meters east-west.<ref name=shimotsuke>[http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002400/hpg000002382.htm Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins] (Shimotsuke City Website).</ref> The temple layout follows the Tōdaiji-style with the South Gate, Middle Gate, Golden Hall, and Lecture Hall aligned in a straight line from south to north. The Middle Gate and Golden Hall are connected by a cloister.<ref name=shimotsuke/> A seven-story pagoda, estimated from the size of its base, stood east of the cloister. Other features include bell towers and sutra repositories between the Golden Hall and Lecture Hall.<ref name=shimotsuke/>
Excavation revealed five distinct phases of development:<ref name=shimotsuke/> * Phase 1: Mid-8th century - Founding period, construction of the pagoda and Golden Hall. * Phase 2: Late 8th to early 9th century - Completion tier 2]][[Category: of major buildings, construction of a wooden-pillar fence around the temple grounds. * Phase 3: Late 9th century - Renovation period, replacement of the wooden-pillar fence with an earthen wall. * Phase 4: From the 10th century - Decline, cessation of repairs to main buildings and moat re-digging. * Phase 5: By the 11th-12th centuries, the temple’s flame of faith was extinguished.
The main features of the temple are: ; Golden Hall : Foundation: 21 meters north-south, 33.6 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 13.8 meters (4 bays) north-south, 25.8 meters (7 bays) east-west. ; Lecture Hall : Foundation: 16.2 meters north-south, 24.6 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 12.6 meters (4 bays) north-south, 21 meters (7 bays) east-west. ; Cloisters ; Bell Tower : Foundation: 12 meters north-south, 9 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 9 meters (3 bays) north-south, 6 meters (2 bays) east-west. ; Sutra Repository : Foundation: 12 meters north-south, 9 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 9 meters (3 bays) north-south, 6 meters (2 bays) east-west. ; Middle Gate : Foundation: 10.2 meters north-south, 22.8 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 2 bays north-south, 5 bays east-west. ; Pagoda : Foundation: 18 meters square. Building dimensions: 3 bays square, estimated height of over 60 meters. A reconstruction model is displayed in the Fudoki Hill Museum. ; South Gate

== Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins == Located approximately 600 meters east of the Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins. The temple site measures 270 meters north-south and 145 meters east-west, with a projecting section on the east side measuring 211 meters north-south and 52 meters east-west.<ref name=shimotsukeniji>[http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002500/hpg000002418.htm Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins] (Shimotsuke City Website).</ref> The layout is similar to that of Shimotsuke Kokubunji, but no pagoda was constructed.<ref name=shimotsukeniji/>
<gallery> File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, kondou.jpg|Golden Hall Ruins File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, koudou.jpg|Lecture Hall Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunniji-ato shourou.JPG|Bell Tower Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunniji-ato kyouzou.JPG|Sutra Repository Ruins File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, chumon-1.jpg|Middle Gate Ruins </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins - Designated on March 3, 1921, with additions to the designated area on March 2, 2005, and March 10, 2015.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|463|Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins}}</ref> * Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins - Designated on April 9, 1965, with an addition on July 28, 1977.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|485|Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}}</ref>
== Location == '''Addresses''' * Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins: {{Coord|36|23|06.21|N|139|48|21.95|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins}} * Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins: {{Coord|36|23|06.98|N|139|48|44.73|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|name=Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}}
'''Related Facilities''' * Tochigi Prefectural Fudoki Hill Museum ** Admission: 100 yen for adults, 50 yen for high school and university students ** Hours: 9:00 AM–5:00 PM (last admission at 4:30 PM) ** Closed: Mondays (open on holidays and Prefectural Day), the day after holidays (open on weekends), year-end and New Year holidays
'''Access''' * Train: [[East Japan Railway Company]] ([[JR East]]) [[Utsunomiya Line]] ([[Tōhoku Main Line]]) [[Koganei Station]] - Approximately 3.5 km ** Bicycle rentals available at the station
'''Nearby''' * {{ill|Shimotsuke Kokubunji|ja|下野国分寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Successor temple. * Kokubun Kofun Cluster - Includes {{ill|Kabutozuka Kofun|ja|甲塚古墳 (下野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Sannōzuka Kofun, Atago Shrine Kofun, and Marutsuka Kofun. * [[:en:Shimotsuke_Provincial_Capital|Shimotsuke Provincial Capital]]
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== References == * On-site explanatory boards * {{Cite book|title=Historical Place Names of Tochigi Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|chapter=Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins and Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimotsuke Kokubunji}} {{Commonscat|Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins|下野国分尼寺跡}} * [http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002400/hpg000002382.htm Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins], [http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002500/hpg000002418.htm Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins] - Shimotsuke City Website * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|463}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|485|Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}} * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/1341002.htm Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins], [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/1341016.htm Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins] - Tochigi Cultural Assets * [http://shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=5 Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins], [http://shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=7 Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins] - Shimotsuke Cultural Heritage
