Building dimensions: 9 meters (3 bays) north-south, 6 meters (2 bays) east-west. ; Sutra Repository : Foundation: 12 meters north-south, 9 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 9 meters (3 bays) north-south, 6 meters (2 bays) east-west. ; Middle Gate : Foundation: 10.2 meters north-south, 22.8 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 2 bays north-south, 5 bays east-west. ; Pagoda : Foundation: 18 meters square. Building dimensions: 3 bays square, estimated height of over 60 meters. A reconstruction model is displayed in the Fudoki Hill Museum. ; South Gate
<gallery> File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato kondou.JPG|Golden Hall Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato koudou.JPG|Lecture Hall Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato soubou.JPG|Cloister Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato shourou.JPG|Bell Tower Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato kyouzou.JPG|Sutra Repository Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato chuumon.JPG|Middle Gate Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato tou.JPG|Pagoda Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato nandaimon.JPG|South Gate Ruins </gallery>
== Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins == Located approximately 600 meters east of the Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins. The temple site measures 270 meters north-south and 145 meters east-west, with a projecting section on the east side measuring 211 meters north-south and 52 meters east-west.<ref name=shimotsukeniji>[http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002500/hpg000002418.htm Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins] (Shimotsuke City Website).</ref> The layout is similar to that of Shimotsuke Kokubunji, but no pagoda was constructed.<ref name=shimotsukeniji/>
<gallery> File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, kondou.jpg|Golden Hall Ruins File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, koudou.jpg|Lecture Hall Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunniji-ato shourou.JPG|Bell Tower Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunniji-ato kyouzou.JPG|Sutra Repository Ruins File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, chumon-1.jpg|Middle Gate Ruins </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins - Designated on March 3, 1921, with additions to the designated area on March 2, 2005, and March 10, 2015.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|463|Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins}}</ref> * Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins - Designated on April 9, 1965, with an addition on July 28, 1977.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|485|Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}}</ref>
== Location == '''Addresses''' * Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins: {{Coord|36|23|06.21|N|139|48|21.95|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins}} * Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins: {{Coord|36|23|06.98|N|139|48|44.73|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|name=Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}}
'''Related Facilities''' * Tochigi Prefectural Fudoki Hill Museum ** Admission: 100 yen for adults, 50 yen for high school and university students ** Hours: 9:00 AM–5:00 PM (last admission at 4:30 PM) ** Closed: Mondays (open on holidays and Prefectural Day), the day after holidays (open on weekends), year-end and New Year holidays
'''Access''' * Train: [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] ([[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]]) [[Utsunomiya Line]] ([[Tōhoku Main Line]]) [[:en:Koganei Station|Koganei Station]] - Approximately 3.5 km ** Bicycle rentals available at the station

revision.text:
[[File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, chumon-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins (National Historic Site)}}]] [[File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, chumon-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins (National Historic Site)}}]] {{GeoGroup}} '''Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins''' (下野国分寺跡) are the remains of an ancient [[:en:Buddhist temples in Japan|Buddhist temples in Japan]] in [[Shimotsuke City]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
It is part of the network of [[provincial temples]] established across Japan by Emperor [[Shōmu]] during the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. The area has been developed as a historic site park.
This article also discusses the '''Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins''', another national historic site. For information about the current temple, see '''[[:en:Shimotsuke Kokubun-ji|Shimotsuke Kokubunji]]'''.
== Overview == The temple ruins are located on a plateau in western [[Shimotsuke City]], between the [[Sugata River]] and the [[Omoi River]] (栃木県). On the opposite bank of the Omoi River, the [[Shimotsuke Kokufu Ruins]] remain. The region, home to some of the largest [[keyhole-shaped tumuli]] (前方後円墳) in Tochigi Prefecture, such as the [[Azuma Kofun]], [[:en:Biwazuka Kofun|Biwazuka Kofun]], and [[Marishitenzuka Kofun]], was a base for the [[Shimotsukeno clan]] during ancient times.
The Kokubunji and Kokubun-niji Ruins are located approximately 600 meters apart from each other, oriented east to west. They have been preserved as part of the '''Tenpyō Hill Park''' (天平の丘公園) within the broader [[Fudoki Hill]] historic site area. The park features a variety of cherry trees, including offspring of the [[Three Great Cherry Trees of Japan]] ([[Jindai Zakura]], [[Usuzumi Zakura]], and [[:en:Miharu Takizakura|Miharu Takizakura]]) and [[Ishiwari Zakura]], as well as many varieties of [[:en:Yaezakura|Yaezakura]]. During cherry blossom season, the area attracts large crowds of flower-viewers.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20220328132917/https://www.ana.co.jp/ja/jp/japan-travel-planner/tochigi/0000017.html Tenpyō Hill Park]. ANA.</ref>
The surrounding forest, mainly consisting of [[konara]] oak, [[:en:kunugi|kunugi]] oak, and [[:en:Zelkova|Zelkova]], represents a prime example of lowland forests in northern [[:en:Kantō|Kantō]], designated as the Kokubunji Greenery Environmental Conservation Area by Tochigi Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.tochigi.lg.jp/d04/30jikannti/09kokubunji.html|title=09 国分寺緑地環境保全地域|website=Tochigi Prefecture|accessdate=2024-11-11}}</ref>
== Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins == The temple site measures 457 meters north-south and 413 meters east-west.<ref name=shimotsuke>[http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002400/hpg000002382.htm Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins] (Shimotsuke City Website).</ref> The temple layout follows the Tōdaiji-style with the South Gate, Middle Gate, Golden Hall, and Lecture Hall aligned in a straight line from south to north. The Middle Gate and Golden Hall are connected by a cloister.<ref name=shimotsuke/> A seven-story pagoda, estimated from the size of its base, stood east of the cloister. Other features include bell towers and sutra repositories between the Golden Hall and Lecture Hall.<ref name=shimotsuke/>
Excavation revealed five distinct phases of development:<ref name=shimotsuke/> * Phase 1: Mid-8th century - Founding period, construction of the pagoda and Golden Hall. * Phase 2: Late 8th to early 9th century - Completion tier 2]][[Category: of major buildings, construction of a wooden-pillar fence around the temple grounds. * Phase 3: Late 9th century - Renovation period, replacement of the wooden-pillar fence with an earthen wall. * Phase 4: From the 10th century - Decline, cessation of repairs to main buildings and moat re-digging. * Phase 5: By the 11th-12th centuries, the temple’s flame of faith was extinguished.
The main features of the temple are: ; Golden Hall : Foundation: 21 meters north-south, 33.6 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 13.8 meters (4 bays) north-south, 25.8 meters (7 bays) east-west. ; Lecture Hall : Foundation: 16.2 meters north-south, 24.6 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 12.6 meters (4 bays) north-south, 21 meters (7 bays) east-west. ; Cloisters ; Bell Tower : Foundation: 12 meters north-south, 9 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 9 meters (3 bays) north-south, 6 meters (2 bays) east-west. ; Sutra Repository : Foundation: 12 meters north-south, 9 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 9 meters (3 bays) north-south, 6 meters (2 bays) east-west. ; Middle Gate : Foundation: 10.2 meters north-south, 22.8 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 2 bays north-south, 5 bays east-west. ; Pagoda : Foundation: 18 meters square. Building dimensions: 3 bays square, estimated height of over 60 meters. A reconstruction model is displayed in the Fudoki Hill Museum. ; South Gate
<gallery> File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato kondou.JPG|Golden Hall Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato koudou.JPG|Lecture Hall Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato soubou.JPG|Cloister Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato shourou.JPG|Bell Tower Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato kyouzou.JPG|Sutra Repository Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato chuumon.JPG|Middle Gate Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato tou.JPG|Pagoda Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato nandaimon.JPG|South Gate Ruins </gallery>
== Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins == Located approximately 600 meters east of the Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins. The temple site measures 270 meters north-south and 145 meters east-west, with a projecting section on the east side measuring 211 meters north-south and 52 meters east-west.<ref name=shimotsukeniji>[http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002500/hpg000002418.htm Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins] (Shimotsuke City Website).</ref> The layout is similar to that of Shimotsuke Kokubunji, but no pagoda was constructed.<ref name=shimotsukeniji/>
<gallery> File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, kondou.jpg|Golden Hall Ruins File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, koudou.jpg|Lecture Hall Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunniji-ato shourou.JPG|Bell Tower Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunniji-ato kyouzou.JPG|Sutra Repository Ruins File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, chumon-1.jpg|Middle Gate Ruins </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins - Designated on March 3, 1921, with additions to the designated area on March 2, 2005, and March 10, 2015.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|463|Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins}}</ref> * Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins - Designated on April 9, 1965, with an addition on July 28, 1977.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|485|Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}}</ref>
== Location == '''Addresses''' * Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins: {{Coord|36|23|06.21|N|139|48|21.95|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins}} * Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins: {{Coord|36|23|06.98|N|139|48|44.73|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|name=Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}}
'''Related Facilities''' * Tochigi Prefectural Fudoki Hill Museum ** Admission: 100 yen for adults, 50 yen for high school and university students ** Hours: 9:00 AM–5:00 PM (last admission at 4:30 PM) ** Closed: Mondays (open on holidays and Prefectural Day), the day after holidays (open on weekends), year-end and New Year holidays
'''Access''' * Train: [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] ([[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]]) [[:en:Utsunomiya Line|Utsunomiya Line]] ([[Tōhoku Main Line]]) [[:en:Koganei Station|Koganei Station]] - Approximately 3.5 km ** Bicycle rentals available at the station
'''Nearby''' * [[:en:Shimotsuke Kokubun-ji|Shimotsuke Kokubunji]] - Successor temple. * Kokubun Kofun Cluster - Includes {{ill|Kabutozuka Kofun|ja|甲塚古墳 (下野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutozuka Kofun|qq=}}, Sannōzuka Kofun, Atago Shrine Kofun, and Marutsuka Kofun. * [[:en:Shimotsuke_Provincial_Capital|Shimotsuke Provincial Capital]]
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== References == * On-site explanatory boards * {{Cite book|title=Historical Place Names of Tochigi Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|chapter=Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins and Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimotsuke Kokubunji}} {{Commonscat|Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins|下野国分尼寺跡}} * [http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002400/hpg000002382.htm Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins], [http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002500/hpg000002418.htm Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins] - Shimotsuke City Website * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|463}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|485|Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}} * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/1341002.htm Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins], [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/1341016.htm Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins] - Tochigi Cultural Assets * [http://shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=5 Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins], [http://shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=7 Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins] - Shimotsuke Cultural Heritage

revision.text:
[[File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, chumon-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins (National Historic Site)}}]] [[File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, chumon-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins (National Historic Site)}}]] {{GeoGroup}} '''Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins''' (下野国分寺跡) are the remains of an ancient [[:en:Buddhist temples in Japan|Buddhist temples in Japan]] in [[Shimotsuke City]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
It is part of the network of [[provincial temples]] established across Japan by Emperor [[Shōmu]] during the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. The area has been developed as a historic site park.
This article also discusses the '''Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins''', another national historic site. For information about the current temple, see '''[[:en:Shimotsuke Kokubun-ji|Shimotsuke Kokubunji]]'''.
== Overview == The temple ruins are located on a plateau in western [[Shimotsuke City]], between the [[Sugata River]] and the [[Omoi River]] (栃木県). On the opposite bank of the Omoi River, the [[Shimotsuke Kokufu Ruins]] remain. The region, home to some of the largest [[keyhole-shaped tumuli]] (前方後円墳) in Tochigi Prefecture, such as the [[Azuma Kofun]], [[:en:Biwazuka Kofun|Biwazuka Kofun]], and [[Marishitenzuka Kofun]], was a base for the [[Shimotsukeno clan]] during ancient times.
The Kokubunji and Kokubun-niji Ruins are located approximately 600 meters apart from each other, oriented east to west. They have been preserved as part of the '''Tenpyō Hill Park''' (天平の丘公園) within the broader [[Fudoki Hill]] historic site area. The park features a variety of cherry trees, including offspring of the [[Three Great Cherry Trees of Japan]] ([[Jindai Zakura]], [[Usuzumi Zakura]], and [[:en:Miharu Takizakura|Miharu Takizakura]]) and [[Ishiwari Zakura]], as well as many varieties of [[:en:Yaezakura|Yaezakura]]. During cherry blossom season, the area attracts large crowds of flower-viewers.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20220328132917/https://www.ana.co.jp/ja/jp/japan-travel-planner/tochigi/0000017.html Tenpyō Hill Park]. ANA.</ref>
The surrounding forest, mainly consisting of [[konara]] oak, [[:en:kunugi|kunugi]] oak, and [[:en:Zelkova|Zelkova]], represents a prime example of lowland forests in northern [[:en:Kantō|Kantō]], designated as the Kokubunji Greenery Environmental Conservation Area by Tochigi Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.tochigi.lg.jp/d04/30jikannti/09kokubunji.html|title=09 国分寺緑地環境保全地域|website=Tochigi Prefecture|accessdate=2024-11-11}}</ref>
== Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins == The temple site measures 457 meters north-south and 413 meters east-west.<ref name=shimotsuke>[http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002400/hpg000002382.htm Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins] (Shimotsuke City Website).</ref> The temple layout follows the Tōdaiji-style with the South Gate, Middle Gate, Golden Hall, and Lecture Hall aligned in a straight line from south to north. The Middle Gate and Golden Hall are connected by a cloister.<ref name=shimotsuke/> A seven-story pagoda, estimated from the size of its base, stood east of the cloister. Other features include bell towers and sutra repositories between the Golden Hall and Lecture Hall.<ref name=shimotsuke/>
Excavation revealed five distinct phases of development:<ref name=shimotsuke/> * Phase 1: Mid-8th century - Founding period, construction of the pagoda and Golden Hall. * Phase 2: Late 8th to early 9th century - Completion tier 2]][[Category: of major buildings, construction of a wooden-pillar fence around the temple grounds. * Phase 3: Late 9th century - Renovation period, replacement of the wooden-pillar fence with an earthen wall. * Phase 4: From the 10th century - Decline, cessation of repairs to main buildings and moat re-digging. * Phase 5: By the 11th-12th centuries, the temple’s flame of faith was extinguished.
The main features of the temple are: ; Golden Hall : Foundation: 21 meters north-south, 33.6 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 13.8 meters (4 bays) north-south, 25.8 meters (7 bays) east-west. ; Lecture Hall : Foundation: 16.2 meters north-south, 24.6 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 12.6 meters (4 bays) north-south, 21 meters (7 bays) east-west. ; Cloisters ; Bell Tower : Foundation: 12 meters north-south, 9 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 9 meters (3 bays) north-south, 6 meters (2 bays) east-west. ; Sutra Repository : Foundation: 12 meters north-south, 9 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 9 meters (3 bays) north-south, 6 meters (2 bays) east-west. ; Middle Gate : Foundation: 10.2 meters north-south, 22.8 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 2 bays north-south, 5 bays east-west. ; Pagoda : Foundation: 18 meters square. Building dimensions: 3 bays square, estimated height of over 60 meters. A reconstruction model is displayed in the Fudoki Hill Museum. ; South Gate
<gallery> File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato kondou.JPG|Golden Hall Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato koudou.JPG|Lecture Hall Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato soubou.JPG|Cloister Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato shourou.JPG|Bell Tower Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato kyouzou.JPG|Sutra Repository Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato chuumon.JPG|Middle Gate Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato tou.JPG|Pagoda Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato nandaimon.JPG|South Gate Ruins </gallery>
== Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins == Located approximately 600 meters east of the Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins. The temple site measures 270 meters north-south and 145 meters east-west, with a projecting section on the east side measuring 211 meters north-south and 52 meters east-west.<ref name=shimotsukeniji>[http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002500/hpg000002418.htm Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins] (Shimotsuke City Website).</ref> The layout is similar to that of Shimotsuke Kokubunji, but no pagoda was constructed.<ref name=shimotsukeniji/>
<gallery> File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, kondou.jpg|Golden Hall Ruins File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, koudou.jpg|Lecture Hall Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunniji-ato shourou.JPG|Bell Tower Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunniji-ato kyouzou.JPG|Sutra Repository Ruins File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, chumon-1.jpg|Middle Gate Ruins </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins - Designated on March 3, 1921, with additions to the designated area on March 2, 2005, and March 10, 2015.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|463|Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins}}</ref> * Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins - Designated on April 9, 1965, with an addition on July 28, 1977.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|485|Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}}</ref>
== Location == '''Addresses''' * Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins: {{Coord|36|23|06.21|N|139|48|21.95|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins}} * Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins: {{Coord|36|23|06.98|N|139|48|44.73|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|name=Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}}
'''Related Facilities''' * Tochigi Prefectural Fudoki Hill Museum ** Admission: 100 yen for adults, 50 yen for high school and university students ** Hours: 9:00 AM–5:00 PM (last admission at 4:30 PM) ** Closed: Mondays (open on holidays and Prefectural Day), the day after holidays (open on weekends), year-end and New Year holidays
'''Access''' * Train: [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] ([[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]]) [[:en:Utsunomiya Line|Utsunomiya Line]] ([[:en:Tōhoku Main Line|Tōhoku Main Line]]) [[:en:Koganei Station|Koganei Station]] - Approximately 3.5 km ** Bicycle rentals available at the station
'''Nearby''' * [[:en:Shimotsuke Kokubun-ji|Shimotsuke Kokubunji]] - Successor temple. * Kokubun Kofun Cluster - Includes {{ill|Kabutozuka Kofun|ja|甲塚古墳 (下野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutozuka Kofun|qq=}}, Sannōzuka Kofun, Atago Shrine Kofun, and Marutsuka Kofun. * [[:en:Shimotsuke_Provincial_Capital|Shimotsuke Provincial Capital]]
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== References == * On-site explanatory boards * {{Cite book|title=Historical Place Names of Tochigi Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|chapter=Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins and Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimotsuke Kokubunji}} {{Commonscat|Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins|下野国分尼寺跡}} * [http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002400/hpg000002382.htm Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins], [http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002500/hpg000002418.htm Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins] - Shimotsuke City Website * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|463}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|485|Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}} * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/1341002.htm Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins], [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/1341016.htm Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins] - Tochigi Cultural Assets * [http://shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=5 Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins], [http://shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=7 Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins] - Shimotsuke Cultural Heritage

Other features include bell towers and sutra repositories between the Golden Hall and Lecture Hall.<ref name=shimotsuke/>
Excavation revealed five distinct phases of development:<ref name=shimotsuke/> * Phase 1: Mid-8th century - Founding period, construction of the pagoda and Golden Hall. * Phase 2: Late 8th to early 9th century - Completion tier 2]][[Category: of major buildings, construction of a wooden-pillar fence around the temple grounds. * Phase 3: Late 9th century - Renovation period, replacement of the wooden-pillar fence with an earthen wall. * Phase 4: From the 10th century - Decline, cessation of repairs to main buildings and moat re-digging. * Phase 5: By the 11th-12th centuries, the temple’s flame of faith was extinguished.

Building dimensions: 13.8 meters (4 bays) north-south, 25.8 meters (7 bays) east-west. ; Lecture Hall : Foundation: 16.2 meters north-south, 24.6 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 12.6 meters (4 bays) north-south, 21 meters (7 bays) east-west. ; Cloisters ; Bell Tower : Foundation: 12 meters north-south, 9 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 9 meters (3 bays) north-south, 6 meters (2 bays) east-west. ; Sutra Repository : Foundation: 12 meters north-south, 9 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 2 bays north-south, 5 bays east-west. ; Pagoda : Foundation: 18 meters square. Building dimensions: 3 bays square, estimated height of over 60 meters. During cherry blossom season, the area attracts large crowds of flower-viewers.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20220328132917/https://www.ana.co.jp/ja/jp/japan-travel-planner/tochigi/0000017.html Tenpyō Hill Park]. ANA.</ref>
The surrounding forest, mainly consisting of [[konara]] oak, [[:en:kunugi|kunugi]] oak, and [[:en:Zelkova|Zelkova]], represents a prime example of lowland forests in northern [[:en:Kantō|Kantō]], designated as the Kokubunji Greenery Environmental Conservation Area by Tochigi Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.tochigi.lg.jp/d04/30jikannti/09kokubunji.html|title=09 国分寺緑地環境保全地域|website=Tochigi Prefecture|accessdate=2024-11-11}}</ref>
== Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins == The temple site measures 457 meters north-south and 413 meters east-west.<ref name=shimotsuke>[http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002400/hpg000002382.htm Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins] (Shimotsuke City Website).</ref> The temple layout follows the Tōdaiji-style with the South Gate, Middle Gate, Golden Hall, and Lecture Hall aligned in a straight line from south to north. The Middle Gate and Golden Hall are connected by a cloister.<ref name=shimotsuke/> A seven-story pagoda, estimated from the size of its base, stood east of the cloister. Other features include bell towers and sutra repositories between the Golden Hall and Lecture Hall.<ref name=shimotsuke/>
Excavation revealed five distinct phases of development:<ref name=shimotsuke/> * Phase 1: Mid-8th century - Founding period, construction of the pagoda and Golden Hall. * Phase 2: Late 8th to early 9th century - Completion tier 2]][[Category: of major buildings, construction of a wooden-pillar fence around the temple grounds. * Phase 3: Late 9th century - Renovation period, replacement of the wooden-pillar fence with an earthen wall. * Phase 4: From the 10th century - Decline, cessation of repairs to main buildings and moat re-digging. * Phase 5: By the 11th-12th centuries, the temple’s flame of faith was extinguished.
The main features of the temple are: ; Golden Hall : Foundation: 21 meters north-south, 33.6 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 13.8 meters (4 bays) north-south, 25.8 meters (7 bays) east-west. ; Lecture Hall : Foundation: 16.2 meters north-south, 24.6 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 12.6 meters (4 bays) north-south, 21 meters (7 bays) east-west. ; Cloisters ; Bell Tower : Foundation: 12 meters north-south, 9 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 9 meters (3 bays) north-south, 6 meters (2 bays) east-west. ; Sutra Repository : Foundation: 12 meters north-south, 9 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 9 meters (3 bays) north-south, 6 meters (2 bays) east-west. ; Middle Gate : Foundation: 10.2 meters north-south, 22.8 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 2 bays north-south, 5 bays east-west. ; Pagoda : Foundation: 18 meters square. Building dimensions: 3 bays square, estimated height of over 60 meters. A reconstruction model is displayed in the Fudoki Hill Museum. ; South Gate
<gallery> File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato kondou.JPG|Golden Hall Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato koudou.JPG|Lecture Hall Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato soubou.JPG|Cloister Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato shourou.JPG|Bell Tower Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato kyouzou.JPG|Sutra Repository Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato chuumon.JPG|Middle Gate Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato tou.JPG|Pagoda Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato nandaimon.JPG|South Gate Ruins </gallery>
== Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins == Located approximately 600 meters east of the Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins. The temple site measures 270 meters north-south and 145 meters east-west, with a projecting section on the east side measuring 211 meters north-south and 52 meters east-west.<ref name=shimotsukeniji>[http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002500/hpg000002418.htm Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins] (Shimotsuke City Website).</ref> The layout is similar to that of Shimotsuke Kokubunji, but no pagoda was constructed.<ref name=shimotsukeniji/>
<gallery> File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, kondou.jpg|Golden Hall Ruins File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, koudou.jpg|Lecture Hall Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunniji-ato shourou.JPG|Bell Tower Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunniji-ato kyouzou.JPG|Sutra Repository Ruins File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, chumon-1.jpg|Middle Gate Ruins </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins - Designated on March 3, 1921, with additions to the designated area on March 2, 2005, and March 10, 2015.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|463|Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins}}</ref> * Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins - Designated on April 9, 1965, with an addition on July 28, 1977.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|485|Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}}</ref>
== Location == '''Addresses''' * Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins: {{Coord|36|23|06.21|N|139|48|21.95|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins}} * Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins: {{Coord|36|23|06.98|N|139|48|44.73|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|name=Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}}
'''Related Facilities''' * Tochigi Prefectural Fudoki Hill Museum ** Admission: 100 yen for adults, 50 yen for high school and university students ** Hours: 9:00 AM–5:00 PM (last admission at 4:30 PM) ** Closed: Mondays (open on holidays and Prefectural Day), the day after holidays (open on weekends), year-end and New Year holidays
'''Access''' * Train: [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] ([[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]]) [[:en:Utsunomiya Line|Utsunomiya Line]] ([[:en:Tōhoku Main Line|Tōhoku Main Line]]) [[:en:Koganei Station|Koganei Station]] - Approximately 3.5 km ** Bicycle rentals available at the station
'''Nearby''' * [[:en:Shimotsuke Kokubun-ji|Shimotsuke Kokubunji]] - Successor temple. * Kokubun Kofun Cluster - Includes {{ill|Kabutozuka Kofun|ja|甲塚古墳 (下野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutozuka Kofun|qq=}}, Sannōzuka Kofun, Atago Shrine Kofun, and Marutsuka Kofun. * [[:en:Shimotsuke_Provincial_Capital|Shimotsuke Provincial Capital]]
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== References == * On-site explanatory boards * {{Cite book|title=Historical Place Names of Tochigi Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|chapter=Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins and Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimotsuke Kokubunji}} {{Commonscat|Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins|下野国分尼寺跡}} * [http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002400/hpg000002382.htm Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins], [http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002500/hpg000002418.htm Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins] - Shimotsuke City Website * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|463}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|485|Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}} * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/1341002.htm Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins], [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/1341016.htm Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins] - Tochigi Cultural Assets * [http://shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=5 Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins], [http://shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=7 Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins] - Shimotsuke Cultural Heritage

revision.text:
[[File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, chumon-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins (National Historic Site)}}]] [[File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, chumon-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins (National Historic Site)}}]] {{GeoGroup}} '''Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins''' (下野国分寺跡) are the remains of an ancient [[:en:Buddhist temples in Japan|Buddhist temples in Japan]] in [[Shimotsuke City]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
It is part of the network of [[provincial temples]] established across Japan by Emperor [[Shōmu]] during the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. The area has been developed as a historic site park.
This article also discusses the '''Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins''', another national historic site. For information about the current temple, see '''[[:en:Shimotsuke Kokubun-ji|Shimotsuke Kokubunji]]'''.
== Overview == The temple ruins are located on a plateau in western [[Shimotsuke City]], between the [[Sugata River]] and the [[Omoi River]] (栃木県). On the opposite bank of the Omoi River, the [[Shimotsuke Kokufu Ruins]] remain. The region, home to some of the largest [[keyhole-shaped tumuli]] (前方後円墳) in Tochigi Prefecture, such as the [[Azuma Kofun]], [[:en:Biwazuka Kofun|Biwazuka Kofun]], and [[Marishitenzuka Kofun]], was a base for the [[Shimotsukeno clan]] during ancient times.
The Kokubunji and Kokubun-niji Ruins are located approximately 600 meters apart from each other, oriented east to west. They have been preserved as part of the '''Tenpyō Hill Park''' (天平の丘公園) within the broader [[Fudoki Hill]] historic site area. The park features a variety of cherry trees, including offspring of the [[Three Great Cherry Trees of Japan]] ([[Jindai Zakura]], [[Usuzumi Zakura]], and [[:en:Miharu Takizakura|Miharu Takizakura]]) and [[Ishiwari Zakura]], as well as many varieties of [[:en:Yaezakura|Yaezakura]]. During cherry blossom season, the area attracts large crowds of flower-viewers.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20220328132917/https://www.ana.co.jp/ja/jp/japan-travel-planner/tochigi/0000017.html Tenpyō Hill Park]. ANA.</ref>
The surrounding forest, mainly consisting of [[konara]] oak, [[:en:kunugi|kunugi]] oak, and [[:en:Zelkova|Zelkova]], represents a prime example of lowland forests in northern [[:en:Kantō|Kantō]], designated as the Kokubunji Greenery Environmental Conservation Area by Tochigi Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.tochigi.lg.jp/d04/30jikannti/09kokubunji.html|title=09 国分寺緑地環境保全地域|website=Tochigi Prefecture|accessdate=2024-11-11}}</ref>
== Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins == The temple site measures 457 meters north-south and 413 meters east-west.<ref name=shimotsuke>[http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002400/hpg000002382.htm Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins] (Shimotsuke City Website).</ref> The temple layout follows the Tōdaiji-style with the South Gate, Middle Gate, Golden Hall, and Lecture Hall aligned in a straight line from south to north. The Middle Gate and Golden Hall are connected by a cloister.<ref name=shimotsuke/> A seven-story pagoda, estimated from the size of its base, stood east of the cloister. Other features include bell towers and sutra repositories between the Golden Hall and Lecture Hall.<ref name=shimotsuke/>
Excavation revealed five distinct phases of development:<ref name=shimotsuke/> * Phase 1: Mid-8th century - Founding period, construction of the pagoda and Golden Hall. * Phase 2: Late 8th to early 9th century - Completion tier 2]][[Category: of major buildings, construction of a wooden-pillar fence around the temple grounds. * Phase 3: Late 9th century - Renovation period, replacement of the wooden-pillar fence with an earthen wall. * Phase 4: From the 10th century - Decline, cessation of repairs to main buildings and moat re-digging. * Phase 5: By the 11th-12th centuries, the temple’s flame of faith was extinguished.
The main features of the temple are: ; Golden Hall : Foundation: 21 meters north-south, 33.6 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 13.8 meters (4 bays) north-south, 25.8 meters (7 bays) east-west. ; Lecture Hall : Foundation: 16.2 meters north-south, 24.6 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 12.6 meters (4 bays) north-south, 21 meters (7 bays) east-west. ; Cloisters ; Bell Tower : Foundation: 12 meters north-south, 9 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 9 meters (3 bays) north-south, 6 meters (2 bays) east-west. ; Sutra Repository : Foundation: 12 meters north-south, 9 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 9 meters (3 bays) north-south, 6 meters (2 bays) east-west. ; Middle Gate : Foundation: 10.2 meters north-south, 22.8 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 2 bays north-south, 5 bays east-west. ; Pagoda : Foundation: 18 meters square. Building dimensions: 3 bays square, estimated height of over 60 meters. A reconstruction model is displayed in the Fudoki Hill Museum. ; South Gate
<gallery> File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato kondou.JPG|Golden Hall Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato koudou.JPG|Lecture Hall Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato soubou.JPG|Cloister Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato shourou.JPG|Bell Tower Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato kyouzou.JPG|Sutra Repository Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato chuumon.JPG|Middle Gate Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato tou.JPG|Pagoda Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato nandaimon.JPG|South Gate Ruins </gallery>
== Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins == Located approximately 600 meters east of the Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins. The temple site measures 270 meters north-south and 145 meters east-west, with a projecting section on the east side measuring 211 meters north-south and 52 meters east-west.<ref name=shimotsukeniji>[http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002500/hpg000002418.htm Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins] (Shimotsuke City Website).</ref> The layout is similar to that of Shimotsuke Kokubunji, but no pagoda was constructed.<ref name=shimotsukeniji/>
<gallery> File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, kondou.jpg|Golden Hall Ruins File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, koudou.jpg|Lecture Hall Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunniji-ato shourou.JPG|Bell Tower Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunniji-ato kyouzou.JPG|Sutra Repository Ruins File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, chumon-1.jpg|Middle Gate Ruins </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins - Designated on March 3, 1921, with additions to the designated area on March 2, 2005, and March 10, 2015.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|463|Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins}}</ref> * Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins - Designated on April 9, 1965, with an addition on July 28, 1977.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|485|Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}}</ref>
== Location == '''Addresses''' * Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins: {{Coord|36|23|06.21|N|139|48|21.95|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins}} * Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins: {{Coord|36|23|06.98|N|139|48|44.73|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|name=Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}}
'''Related Facilities''' * Tochigi Prefectural Fudoki Hill Museum ** Admission: 100 yen for adults, 50 yen for high school and university students ** Hours: 9:00 AM–5:00 PM (last admission at 4:30 PM) ** Closed: Mondays (open on holidays and Prefectural Day), the day after holidays (open on weekends), year-end and New Year holidays
'''Access''' * Train: [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] ([[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]]) [[:en:Utsunomiya Line|Utsunomiya Line]] ([[:en:Tōhoku Main Line|Tōhoku Main Line]]) [[:en:Koganei Station|Koganei Station]] - Approximately 3.5 km ** Bicycle rentals available at the station
'''Nearby''' * [[:en:Shimotsuke Kokubun-ji|Shimotsuke Kokubunji]] - Successor temple. * Kokubun Kofun Cluster - Includes {{ill|Kabutozuka Kofun|ja|甲塚古墳 (下野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutozuka Kofun|qq=}}, Sannōzuka Kofun, Atago Shrine Kofun, and Marutsuka Kofun. * [[:en:Shimotsuke_Provincial_Capital|Shimotsuke Provincial Capital]]
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== References == * On-site explanatory boards * {{Cite book|title=Historical Place Names of Tochigi Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|chapter=Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins and Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimotsuke Kokubunji}} {{Commonscat|Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins|下野国分尼寺跡}} * [http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002400/hpg000002382.htm Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins], [http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002500/hpg000002418.htm Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins] - Shimotsuke City Website * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|463}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|485|Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}} * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/1341002.htm Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins], [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/1341016.htm Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins] - Tochigi Cultural Assets * [http://shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=5 Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins], [http://shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=7 Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins] - Shimotsuke Cultural Heritage

ANA.</ref>

Other features include bell towers and sutra repositories between the Golden Hall and Lecture Hall.<ref name=shimotsuke/>
Excavation revealed five distinct phases of development:<ref name=shimotsuke/> * Phase 1: Mid-8th century - Founding period, construction of the pagoda and Golden Hall. * Phase 2: Late 8th to early 9th century - Completion tier 2]][[Category: of major buildings, construction of a wooden-pillar fence around the temple grounds. * Phase 3: Late 9th century - Renovation period, replacement of the wooden-pillar fence with an earthen wall. * Phase 4: From the 10th century - Decline, cessation of repairs to main buildings and moat re-digging. * Phase 5: By the 11th-12th centuries, the temple’s flame of faith was extinguished.

The temple site measures 270 meters north-south and 145 meters east-west, with a projecting section on the east side measuring 211 meters north-south and 52 meters east-west.<ref name=shimotsukeniji>[http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002500/hpg000002418.htm Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins] (Shimotsuke City Website).</ref> The layout is similar to that of Shimotsuke Kokubunji, but no pagoda was constructed.<ref name=shimotsukeniji/>
<gallery> File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, kondou.jpg|Golden Hall Ruins File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, koudou.jpg|Lecture Hall Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunniji-ato shourou.JPG|Bell Tower Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunniji-ato kyouzou.JPG|Sutra Repository Ruins File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, chumon-1.jpg|Middle Gate Ruins </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins - Designated on March 3, 1921, with additions to the designated area on March 2, 2005, and March 10, 2015.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|463|Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins}}</ref> * Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins - Designated on April 9, 1965, with an addition on July 28, 1977.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|485|Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}}</ref>
== Location == '''Addresses''' * Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins: {{Coord|36|23|06.21|N|139|48|21.95|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins}} * Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins: {{Coord|36|23|06.98|N|139|48|44.73|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|name=Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}}
'''Related Facilities''' * Tochigi Prefectural Fudoki Hill Museum ** Admission: 100 yen for adults, 50 yen for high school and university students ** Hours: 9:00 AM–5:00 PM (last admission at 4:30 PM) ** Closed: Mondays (open on holidays and Prefectural Day), the day after holidays (open on weekends), year-end and New Year holidays
'''Access''' * Train: [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] ([[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]]) [[:en:Utsunomiya Line|Utsunomiya Line]] ([[:en:Tōhoku Main Line|Tōhoku Main Line]]) [[:en:Koganei Station|Koganei Station]] - Approximately 3.5 km ** Bicycle rentals available at the station
'''Nearby''' * [[:en:Shimotsuke Kokubun-ji|Shimotsuke Kokubunji]] - Successor temple. * Kokubun Kofun Cluster - Includes {{ill|Kabutozuka Kofun|ja|甲塚古墳 (下野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutozuka Kofun|qq=}}, Sannōzuka Kofun, Atago Shrine Kofun, and Marutsuka Kofun. * [[:en:Shimotsuke_Provincial_Capital|Shimotsuke Provincial Capital]]
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== References == * On-site explanatory boards * {{Cite book|title=Historical Place Names of Tochigi Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|chapter=Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins and Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimotsuke Kokubunji}} {{Commonscat|Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins|下野国分尼寺跡}} * [http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002400/hpg000002382.htm Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins], [http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002500/hpg000002418.htm Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins] - Shimotsuke City Website * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|463}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|485|Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}} * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/1341002.htm Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins], [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/1341016.htm Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins] - Tochigi Cultural Assets * [http://shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=5 Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins], [http://shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=7 Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins] - Shimotsuke Cultural Heritage

revision.comment: Bot: add wikidata link templates
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11361382]] Wikidata name: Shimotsuke kokubun-ji ato [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, chumon-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins (National Historic Site)}}]] [[File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, chumon-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins (National Historic Site)}}]] {{GeoGroup}} '''Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins''' (下野国分寺跡) are the remains of an ancient [[:en:Buddhist temples in Japan|Buddhist temples in Japan]] in [[Shimotsuke City]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
It is part of the network of [[provincial temples]] established across Japan by Emperor [[Shōmu]] during the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. The area has been developed as a historic site park.
This article also discusses the '''Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins''', another national historic site. For information about the current temple, see '''[[:en:Shimotsuke Kokubun-ji|Shimotsuke Kokubunji]]'''.
== Overview == The temple ruins are located on a plateau in western [[Shimotsuke City]], between the [[Sugata River]] and the [[Omoi River]] (栃木県). On the opposite bank of the Omoi River, the [[Shimotsuke Kokufu Ruins]] remain. The region, home to some of the largest [[keyhole-shaped tumuli]] (前方後円墳) in Tochigi Prefecture, such as the [[Azuma Kofun]], [[:en:Biwazuka Kofun|Biwazuka Kofun]], and [[Marishitenzuka Kofun]], was a base for the [[Shimotsukeno clan]] during ancient times.
The Kokubunji and Kokubun-niji Ruins are located approximately 600 meters apart from each other, oriented east to west. They have been preserved as part of the '''Tenpyō Hill Park''' (天平の丘公園) within the broader [[Fudoki Hill]] historic site area. The park features a variety of cherry trees, including offspring of the [[Three Great Cherry Trees of Japan]] ([[Jindai Zakura]], [[Usuzumi Zakura]], and [[:en:Miharu Takizakura|Miharu Takizakura]]) and [[Ishiwari Zakura]], as well as many varieties of [[:en:Yaezakura|Yaezakura]]. During cherry blossom season, the area attracts large crowds of flower-viewers.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20220328132917/https://www.ana.co.jp/ja/jp/japan-travel-planner/tochigi/0000017.html Tenpyō Hill Park]. ANA.</ref>
The surrounding forest, mainly consisting of [[konara]] oak, [[:en:kunugi|kunugi]] oak, and [[:en:Zelkova|Zelkova]], represents a prime example of lowland forests in northern [[:en:Kantō|Kantō]], designated as the Kokubunji Greenery Environmental Conservation Area by Tochigi Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.tochigi.lg.jp/d04/30jikannti/09kokubunji.html|title=09 国分寺緑地環境保全地域|website=Tochigi Prefecture|accessdate=2024-11-11}}</ref>
== Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins == The temple site measures 457 meters north-south and 413 meters east-west.<ref name=shimotsuke>[http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002400/hpg000002382.htm Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins] (Shimotsuke City Website).</ref> The temple layout follows the Tōdaiji-style with the South Gate, Middle Gate, Golden Hall, and Lecture Hall aligned in a straight line from south to north. The Middle Gate and Golden Hall are connected by a cloister.<ref name=shimotsuke/> A seven-story pagoda, estimated from the size of its base, stood east of the cloister. Other features include bell towers and sutra repositories between the Golden Hall and Lecture Hall.<ref name=shimotsuke/>
Excavation revealed five distinct phases of development:<ref name=shimotsuke/> * Phase 1: Mid-8th century - Founding period, construction of the pagoda and Golden Hall. * Phase 2: Late 8th to early 9th century - Completion tier 2]][[Category: of major buildings, construction of a wooden-pillar fence around the temple grounds. * Phase 3: Late 9th century - Renovation period, replacement of the wooden-pillar fence with an earthen wall. * Phase 4: From the 10th century - Decline, cessation of repairs to main buildings and moat re-digging. * Phase 5: By the 11th-12th centuries, the temple’s flame of faith was extinguished.
The main features of the temple are: ; Golden Hall : Foundation: 21 meters north-south, 33.6 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 13.8 meters (4 bays) north-south, 25.8 meters (7 bays) east-west. ; Lecture Hall : Foundation: 16.2 meters north-south, 24.6 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 12.6 meters (4 bays) north-south, 21 meters (7 bays) east-west. ; Cloisters ; Bell Tower : Foundation: 12 meters north-south, 9 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 9 meters (3 bays) north-south, 6 meters (2 bays) east-west. ; Sutra Repository : Foundation: 12 meters north-south, 9 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 9 meters (3 bays) north-south, 6 meters (2 bays) east-west. ; Middle Gate : Foundation: 10.2 meters north-south, 22.8 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 2 bays north-south, 5 bays east-west. ; Pagoda : Foundation: 18 meters square. Building dimensions: 3 bays square, estimated height of over 60 meters. A reconstruction model is displayed in the Fudoki Hill Museum. ; South Gate
<gallery> File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato kondou.JPG|Golden Hall Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato koudou.JPG|Lecture Hall Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato soubou.JPG|Cloister Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato shourou.JPG|Bell Tower Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato kyouzou.JPG|Sutra Repository Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato chuumon.JPG|Middle Gate Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato tou.JPG|Pagoda Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato nandaimon.JPG|South Gate Ruins </gallery>
== Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins == Located approximately 600 meters east of the Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins. The temple site measures 270 meters north-south and 145 meters east-west, with a projecting section on the east side measuring 211 meters north-south and 52 meters east-west.<ref name=shimotsukeniji>[http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002500/hpg000002418.htm Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins] (Shimotsuke City Website).</ref> The layout is similar to that of Shimotsuke Kokubunji, but no pagoda was constructed.<ref name=shimotsukeniji/>
<gallery> File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, kondou.jpg|Golden Hall Ruins File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, koudou.jpg|Lecture Hall Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunniji-ato shourou.JPG|Bell Tower Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunniji-ato kyouzou.JPG|Sutra Repository Ruins File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, chumon-1.jpg|Middle Gate Ruins </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins - Designated on March 3, 1921, with additions to the designated area on March 2, 2005, and March 10, 2015.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|463|Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins}}</ref> * Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins - Designated on April 9, 1965, with an addition on July 28, 1977.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|485|Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}}</ref>
== Location == '''Addresses''' * Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins: {{Coord|36|23|06.21|N|139|48|21.95|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins}} * Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins: {{Coord|36|23|06.98|N|139|48|44.73|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|name=Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}}
'''Related Facilities''' * Tochigi Prefectural Fudoki Hill Museum ** Admission: 100 yen for adults, 50 yen for high school and university students ** Hours: 9:00 AM–5:00 PM (last admission at 4:30 PM) ** Closed: Mondays (open on holidays and Prefectural Day), the day after holidays (open on weekends), year-end and New Year holidays
'''Access''' * Train: [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] ([[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]]) [[:en:Utsunomiya Line|Utsunomiya Line]] ([[:en:Tōhoku Main Line|Tōhoku Main Line]]) [[:en:Koganei Station|Koganei Station]] - Approximately 3.5 km ** Bicycle rentals available at the station
'''Nearby''' * [[:en:Shimotsuke Kokubun-ji|Shimotsuke Kokubunji]] - Successor temple. * Kokubun Kofun Cluster - Includes {{ill|Kabutozuka Kofun|ja|甲塚古墳 (下野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutozuka Kofun|qq=}}, Sannōzuka Kofun, Atago Shrine Kofun, and Marutsuka Kofun. * [[:en:Shimotsuke_Provincial_Capital|Shimotsuke Provincial Capital]]
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== References == * On-site explanatory boards * {{Cite book|title=Historical Place Names of Tochigi Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|chapter=Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins and Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimotsuke Kokubunji}} {{Commonscat|Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins|下野国分尼寺跡}} * [http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002400/hpg000002382.htm Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins], [http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002500/hpg000002418.htm Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins] - Shimotsuke City Website * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|463}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|485|Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}} * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/1341002.htm Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins], [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/1341016.htm Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins] - Tochigi Cultural Assets * [http://shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=5 Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins], [http://shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=7 Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins] - Shimotsuke Cultural Heritage

It is part of the network of [[provincial temples]] established across Japan by Emperor [[Shōmu]] during the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. The area has been developed as a historic site park.
This article also discusses the '''Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins''', another national historic site. For information about the current temple, see '''[[:en:Shimotsuke Kokubun-ji|Shimotsuke Kokubunji]]'''.

On the opposite bank of the Omoi River, the [[Shimotsuke Kokufu Ruins]] remain. The region, home to some of the largest [[keyhole-shaped tumuli]] (前方後円墳) in Tochigi Prefecture, such as the [[Azuma Kofun]], [[:en:Biwazuka Kofun|Biwazuka Kofun]], and [[Marishitenzuka Kofun]], was a base for the [[Shimotsukeno clan]] during ancient times.
The Kokubunji and Kokubun-niji Ruins are located approximately 600 meters apart from each other, oriented east to west. They have been preserved as part of the '''Tenpyō Hill Park''' (天平の丘公園) within the broader [[Fudoki Hill]] historic site area. The park features a variety of cherry trees, including offspring of the [[Three Great Cherry Trees of Japan]] ([[Jindai Zakura]], [[Usuzumi Zakura]], and [[:en:Miharu Takizakura|Miharu Takizakura]]) and [[Ishiwari Zakura]], as well as many varieties of [[:en:Yaezakura|Yaezakura]]. During cherry blossom season, the area attracts large crowds of flower-viewers.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20220328132917/https://www.ana.co.jp/ja/jp/japan-travel-planner/tochigi/0000017.html Tenpyō Hill Park]. ANA.</ref>
The surrounding forest, mainly consisting of [[konara]] oak, [[:en:kunugi|kunugi]] oak, and [[:en:Zelkova|Zelkova]], represents a prime example of lowland forests in northern [[:en:Kantō|Kantō]], designated as the Kokubunji Greenery Environmental Conservation Area by Tochigi Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.tochigi.lg.jp/d04/30jikannti/09kokubunji.html|title=09 国分寺緑地環境保全地域|website=Tochigi Prefecture|accessdate=2024-11-11}}</ref>
== Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins == The temple site measures 457 meters north-south and 413 meters east-west.<ref name=shimotsuke>[http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002400/hpg000002382.htm Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins] (Shimotsuke City Website).</ref> The temple layout follows the Tōdaiji-style with the South Gate, Middle Gate, Golden Hall, and Lecture Hall aligned in a straight line from south to north. The Middle Gate and Golden Hall are connected by a cloister.<ref name=shimotsuke/> A seven-story pagoda, estimated from the size of its base, stood east of the cloister. Other features include bell towers and sutra repositories between the Golden Hall and Lecture Hall.<ref name=shimotsuke/>
Excavation revealed five distinct phases of development:<ref name=shimotsuke/> * Phase 1: Mid-8th century - Founding period, construction of the pagoda and Golden Hall. * Phase 2: Late 8th to early 9th century - Completion tier 2]][[Category: of major buildings, construction of a wooden-pillar fence around the temple grounds. * Phase 3: Late 9th century - Renovation period, replacement of the wooden-pillar fence with an earthen wall. * Phase 4: From the 10th century - Decline, cessation of repairs to main buildings and moat re-digging. * Phase 5: By the 11th-12th centuries, the temple’s flame of faith was extinguished.
The main features of the temple are: ; Golden Hall : Foundation: 21 meters north-south, 33.6 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 13.8 meters (4 bays) north-south, 25.8 meters (7 bays) east-west. ; Lecture Hall : Foundation: 16.2 meters north-south, 24.6 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 12.6 meters (4 bays) north-south, 21 meters (7 bays) east-west. ; Cloisters ; Bell Tower : Foundation: 12 meters north-south, 9 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 9 meters (3 bays) north-south, 6 meters (2 bays) east-west. ; Sutra Repository : Foundation: 12 meters north-south, 9 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 9 meters (3 bays) north-south, 6 meters (2 bays) east-west. ; Middle Gate : Foundation: 10.2 meters north-south, 22.8 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 2 bays north-south, 5 bays east-west. ; Pagoda : Foundation: 18 meters square. Building dimensions: 3 bays square, estimated height of over 60 meters. A reconstruction model is displayed in the Fudoki Hill Museum. ; South Gate

revision.comment: Bot: add interwikis from Wikidata
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Remains of Shimotsuke-Kokubunji Temple]][[ja:下野国分寺跡]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11361382]] Wikidata name: Shimotsuke kokubun-ji ato [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, chumon-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins (National Historic Site)}}]] [[File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, chumon-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins (National Historic Site)}}]] {{GeoGroup}} '''Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins''' (下野国分寺跡) are the remains of an ancient [[:en:Buddhist temples in Japan|Buddhist temples in Japan]] in [[Shimotsuke City]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
It is part of the network of [[provincial temples]] established across Japan by Emperor [[Shōmu]] during the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. The area has been developed as a historic site park.
This article also discusses the '''Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins''', another national historic site. For information about the current temple, see '''[[:en:Shimotsuke Kokubun-ji|Shimotsuke Kokubunji]]'''.
== Overview == The temple ruins are located on a plateau in western [[Shimotsuke City]], between the [[Sugata River]] and the [[Omoi River]] (栃木県). On the opposite bank of the Omoi River, the [[Shimotsuke Kokufu Ruins]] remain. The region, home to some of the largest [[keyhole-shaped tumuli]] (前方後円墳) in Tochigi Prefecture, such as the [[Azuma Kofun]], [[:en:Biwazuka Kofun|Biwazuka Kofun]], and [[Marishitenzuka Kofun]], was a base for the [[Shimotsukeno clan]] during ancient times.
The Kokubunji and Kokubun-niji Ruins are located approximately 600 meters apart from each other, oriented east to west. They have been preserved as part of the '''Tenpyō Hill Park''' (天平の丘公園) within the broader [[Fudoki Hill]] historic site area. The park features a variety of cherry trees, including offspring of the [[Three Great Cherry Trees of Japan]] ([[Jindai Zakura]], [[Usuzumi Zakura]], and [[:en:Miharu Takizakura|Miharu Takizakura]]) and [[Ishiwari Zakura]], as well as many varieties of [[:en:Yaezakura|Yaezakura]]. During cherry blossom season, the area attracts large crowds of flower-viewers.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20220328132917/https://www.ana.co.jp/ja/jp/japan-travel-planner/tochigi/0000017.html Tenpyō Hill Park]. ANA.</ref>
The surrounding forest, mainly consisting of [[konara]] oak, [[:en:kunugi|kunugi]] oak, and [[:en:Zelkova|Zelkova]], represents a prime example of lowland forests in northern [[:en:Kantō|Kantō]], designated as the Kokubunji Greenery Environmental Conservation Area by Tochigi Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.tochigi.lg.jp/d04/30jikannti/09kokubunji.html|title=09 国分寺緑地環境保全地域|website=Tochigi Prefecture|accessdate=2024-11-11}}</ref>
== Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins == The temple site measures 457 meters north-south and 413 meters east-west.<ref name=shimotsuke>[http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002400/hpg000002382.htm Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins] (Shimotsuke City Website).</ref> The temple layout follows the Tōdaiji-style with the South Gate, Middle Gate, Golden Hall, and Lecture Hall aligned in a straight line from south to north. The Middle Gate and Golden Hall are connected by a cloister.<ref name=shimotsuke/> A seven-story pagoda, estimated from the size of its base, stood east of the cloister. Other features include bell towers and sutra repositories between the Golden Hall and Lecture Hall.<ref name=shimotsuke/>
Excavation revealed five distinct phases of development:<ref name=shimotsuke/> * Phase 1: Mid-8th century - Founding period, construction of the pagoda and Golden Hall. * Phase 2: Late 8th to early 9th century - Completion tier 2]][[Category: of major buildings, construction of a wooden-pillar fence around the temple grounds. * Phase 3: Late 9th century - Renovation period, replacement of the wooden-pillar fence with an earthen wall. * Phase 4: From the 10th century - Decline, cessation of repairs to main buildings and moat re-digging. * Phase 5: By the 11th-12th centuries, the temple’s flame of faith was extinguished.
The main features of the temple are: ; Golden Hall : Foundation: 21 meters north-south, 33.6 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 13.8 meters (4 bays) north-south, 25.8 meters (7 bays) east-west. ; Lecture Hall : Foundation: 16.2 meters north-south, 24.6 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 12.6 meters (4 bays) north-south, 21 meters (7 bays) east-west. ; Cloisters ; Bell Tower : Foundation: 12 meters north-south, 9 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 9 meters (3 bays) north-south, 6 meters (2 bays) east-west. ; Sutra Repository : Foundation: 12 meters north-south, 9 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 9 meters (3 bays) north-south, 6 meters (2 bays) east-west. ; Middle Gate : Foundation: 10.2 meters north-south, 22.8 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 2 bays north-south, 5 bays east-west. ; Pagoda : Foundation: 18 meters square. Building dimensions: 3 bays square, estimated height of over 60 meters. A reconstruction model is displayed in the Fudoki Hill Museum. ; South Gate
<gallery> File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato kondou.JPG|Golden Hall Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato koudou.JPG|Lecture Hall Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato soubou.JPG|Cloister Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato shourou.JPG|Bell Tower Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato kyouzou.JPG|Sutra Repository Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato chuumon.JPG|Middle Gate Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato tou.JPG|Pagoda Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato nandaimon.JPG|South Gate Ruins </gallery>
== Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins == Located approximately 600 meters east of the Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins. The temple site measures 270 meters north-south and 145 meters east-west, with a projecting section on the east side measuring 211 meters north-south and 52 meters east-west.<ref name=shimotsukeniji>[http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002500/hpg000002418.htm Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins] (Shimotsuke City Website).</ref> The layout is similar to that of Shimotsuke Kokubunji, but no pagoda was constructed.<ref name=shimotsukeniji/>
<gallery> File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, kondou.jpg|Golden Hall Ruins File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, koudou.jpg|Lecture Hall Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunniji-ato shourou.JPG|Bell Tower Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunniji-ato kyouzou.JPG|Sutra Repository Ruins File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, chumon-1.jpg|Middle Gate Ruins </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins - Designated on March 3, 1921, with additions to the designated area on March 2, 2005, and March 10, 2015.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|463|Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins}}</ref> * Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins - Designated on April 9, 1965, with an addition on July 28, 1977.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|485|Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}}</ref>
== Location == '''Addresses''' * Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins: {{Coord|36|23|06.21|N|139|48|21.95|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins}} * Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins: {{Coord|36|23|06.98|N|139|48|44.73|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|name=Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}}
'''Related Facilities''' * Tochigi Prefectural Fudoki Hill Museum ** Admission: 100 yen for adults, 50 yen for high school and university students ** Hours: 9:00 AM–5:00 PM (last admission at 4:30 PM) ** Closed: Mondays (open on holidays and Prefectural Day), the day after holidays (open on weekends), year-end and New Year holidays
'''Access''' * Train: [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] ([[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]]) [[:en:Utsunomiya Line|Utsunomiya Line]] ([[:en:Tōhoku Main Line|Tōhoku Main Line]]) [[:en:Koganei Station|Koganei Station]] - Approximately 3.5 km ** Bicycle rentals available at the station
'''Nearby''' * [[:en:Shimotsuke Kokubun-ji|Shimotsuke Kokubunji]] - Successor temple. * Kokubun Kofun Cluster - Includes {{ill|Kabutozuka Kofun|ja|甲塚古墳 (下野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutozuka Kofun|qq=}}, Sannōzuka Kofun, Atago Shrine Kofun, and Marutsuka Kofun. * [[:en:Shimotsuke_Provincial_Capital|Shimotsuke Provincial Capital]]
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== References == * On-site explanatory boards * {{Cite book|title=Historical Place Names of Tochigi Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|chapter=Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins and Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimotsuke Kokubunji}} {{Commonscat|Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins|下野国分尼寺跡}} * [http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002400/hpg000002382.htm Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins], [http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002500/hpg000002418.htm Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins] - Shimotsuke City Website * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|463}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|485|Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}} * [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/1341002.htm Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins], [http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/1341016.htm Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins] - Tochigi Cultural Assets * [http://shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=5 Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins], [http://shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=7 Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins] - Shimotsuke Cultural Heritage

It is part of the network of [[provincial temples]] established across Japan by Emperor [[Shōmu]] during the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. The area has been developed as a historic site park.
This article also discusses the '''Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins''', another national historic site. For information about the current temple, see '''[[:en:Shimotsuke Kokubun-ji|Shimotsuke Kokubunji]]'''.

The Kokubunji and Kokubun-niji Ruins are located approximately 600 meters apart from each other, oriented east to west. During cherry blossom season, the area attracts large crowds of flower-viewers.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20220328132917/https://www.ana.co.jp/ja/jp/japan-travel-planner/tochigi/0000017.html Tenpyō Hill Park]. ANA.</ref>
The surrounding forest, mainly consisting of [[konara]] oak, [[:en:kunugi|kunugi]] oak, and [[:en:Zelkova|Zelkova]], represents a prime example of lowland forests in northern [[:en:Kantō|Kantō]], designated as the Kokubunji Greenery Environmental Conservation Area by Tochigi Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.tochigi.lg.jp/d04/30jikannti/09kokubunji.html|title=09 国分寺緑地環境保全地域|website=Tochigi Prefecture|accessdate=2024-11-11}}</ref>
== Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins == The temple site measures 457 meters north-south and 413 meters east-west.<ref name=shimotsuke>[http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002400/hpg000002382.htm Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins] (Shimotsuke City Website).</ref> The temple layout follows the Tōdaiji-style with the South Gate, Middle Gate, Golden Hall, and Lecture Hall aligned in a straight line from south to north. The Middle Gate and Golden Hall are connected by a cloister.<ref name=shimotsuke/> A seven-story pagoda, estimated from the size of its base, stood east of the cloister. Other features include bell towers and sutra repositories between the Golden Hall and Lecture Hall.<ref name=shimotsuke/>
Excavation revealed five distinct phases of development:<ref name=shimotsuke/> * Phase 1: Mid-8th century - Founding period, construction of the pagoda and Golden Hall. * Phase 2: Late 8th to early 9th century - Completion tier 2]][[Category: of major buildings, construction of a wooden-pillar fence around the temple grounds. * Phase 3: Late 9th century - Renovation period, replacement of the wooden-pillar fence with an earthen wall. * Phase 4: From the 10th century - Decline, cessation of repairs to main buildings and moat re-digging. * Phase 5: By the 11th-12th centuries, the temple’s flame of faith was extinguished.
The main features of the temple are: ; Golden Hall : Foundation: 21 meters north-south, 33.6 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 13.8 meters (4 bays) north-south, 25.8 meters (7 bays) east-west. ; Lecture Hall : Foundation: 16.2 meters north-south, 24.6 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 12.6 meters (4 bays) north-south, 21 meters (7 bays) east-west. ; Cloisters ; Bell Tower : Foundation: 12 meters north-south, 9 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 9 meters (3 bays) north-south, 6 meters (2 bays) east-west. ; Sutra Repository : Foundation: 12 meters north-south, 9 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 9 meters (3 bays) north-south, 6 meters (2 bays) east-west. ; Middle Gate : Foundation: 10.2 meters north-south, 22.8 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 2 bays north-south, 5 bays east-west. ; Pagoda : Foundation: 18 meters square. Building dimensions: 3 bays square, estimated height of over 60 meters. A reconstruction model is displayed in the Fudoki Hill Museum. ; South Gate

The temple site measures 270 meters north-south and 145 meters east-west, with a projecting section on the east side measuring 211 meters north-south and 52 meters east-west.<ref name=shimotsukeniji>[http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002500/hpg000002418.htm Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins] (Shimotsuke City Website).</ref> The layout is similar to that of Shimotsuke Kokubunji, but no pagoda was constructed.<ref name=shimotsukeniji/>

== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins - Designated on March 3, 1921, with additions to the designated area on March 2, 2005, and March 10, 2015.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|463|Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins}}</ref> * Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins - Designated on April 9, 1965, with an addition on July 28, 1977.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|485|Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}}</ref>
== Location == '''Addresses''' * Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins: {{Coord|36|23|06.21|N|139|48|21.95|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins}} * Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins: {{Coord|36|23|06.98|N|139|48|44.73|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|name=Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}}
'''Related Facilities''' * Tochigi Prefectural Fudoki Hill Museum ** Admission: 100 yen for adults, 50 yen for high school and university students ** Hours: 9:00 AM–5:00 PM (last admission at 4:30 PM) ** Closed: Mondays (open on holidays and Prefectural Day), the day after holidays (open on weekends), year-end and New Year holidays

'''Nearby''' * [[:en:Shimotsuke Kokubun-ji|Shimotsuke Kokubunji]] - Successor temple. * Kokubun Kofun Cluster - Includes {{ill|Kabutozuka Kofun|ja|甲塚古墳 (下野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutozuka Kofun|qq=}}, Sannōzuka Kofun, Atago Shrine Kofun, and Marutsuka Kofun. * [[:en:Shimotsuke_Provincial_Capital|Shimotsuke Provincial Capital]]

The surrounding forest, mainly consisting of [[konara]] oak, [[:en:kunugi|kunugi]] oak, and [[:en:Zelkova|Zelkova]], represents a prime example of lowland forests in northern [[:en:Kantō|Kantō]], designated as the Kokubunji Greenery Environmental Conservation Area by Tochigi Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.tochigi.lg.jp/d04/30jikannti/09kokubunji.html|title=09 国分寺緑地環境保全地域|website=Tochigi Prefecture|accessdate=2024-11-11}}</ref>
== Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins == The temple site measures 457 meters north-south and 413 meters east-west.<ref name=shimotsuke>[http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002400/hpg000002382.htm Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins] (Shimotsuke City Website).</ref> The temple layout follows the Tōdaiji-style with the South Gate, Middle Gate, Golden Hall, and Lecture Hall aligned in a straight line from south to north. The Middle Gate and Golden Hall are connected by a cloister.<ref name=shimotsuke/> A seven-story pagoda, estimated from the size of its base, stood east of the cloister. Other features include bell towers and sutra repositories between the Golden Hall and Lecture Hall.<ref name=shimotsuke/>
Excavation revealed five distinct phases of development:<ref name=shimotsuke/> * Phase 1: Mid-8th century - Founding period, construction of the pagoda and Golden Hall. * Phase 2: Late 8th to early 9th century - Completion tier 2]][[Category: of major buildings, construction of a wooden-pillar fence around the temple grounds. * Phase 3: Late 9th century - Renovation period, replacement of the wooden-pillar fence with an earthen wall. * Phase 4: From the 10th century - Decline, cessation of repairs to main buildings and moat re-digging. * Phase 5: By the 11th-12th centuries, the temple’s flame of faith was extinguished.
The main features of the temple are: ; Golden Hall : Foundation: 21 meters north-south, 33.6 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 13.8 meters (4 bays) north-south, 25.8 meters (7 bays) east-west. ; Lecture Hall : Foundation: 16.2 meters north-south, 24.6 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 12.6 meters (4 bays) north-south, 21 meters (7 bays) east-west. ; Cloisters ; Bell Tower : Foundation: 12 meters north-south, 9 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 9 meters (3 bays) north-south, 6 meters (2 bays) east-west. ; Sutra Repository : Foundation: 12 meters north-south, 9 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 9 meters (3 bays) north-south, 6 meters (2 bays) east-west. ; Middle Gate : Foundation: 10.2 meters north-south, 22.8 meters east-west. Building dimensions: 2 bays north-south, 5 bays east-west. ; Pagoda : Foundation: 18 meters square. Building dimensions: 3 bays square, estimated height of over 60 meters. A reconstruction model is displayed in the Fudoki Hill Museum. ; South Gate
<gallery> File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato kondou.JPG|Golden Hall Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato koudou.JPG|Lecture Hall Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato soubou.JPG|Cloister Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato shourou.JPG|Bell Tower Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato kyouzou.JPG|Sutra Repository Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato chuumon.JPG|Middle Gate Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato tou.JPG|Pagoda Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunji-ato nandaimon.JPG|South Gate Ruins </gallery>
== Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins == Located approximately 600 meters east of the Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins. The temple site measures 270 meters north-south and 145 meters east-west, with a projecting section on the east side measuring 211 meters north-south and 52 meters east-west.<ref name=shimotsukeniji>[http://www.city.shimotsuke.lg.jp/hp/page000002500/hpg000002418.htm Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins] (Shimotsuke City Website).</ref> The layout is similar to that of Shimotsuke Kokubunji, but no pagoda was constructed.<ref name=shimotsukeniji/>
<gallery> File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, kondou.jpg|Golden Hall Ruins File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, koudou.jpg|Lecture Hall Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunniji-ato shourou.JPG|Bell Tower Ruins File:Shimotsuke-kokubunniji-ato kyouzou.JPG|Sutra Repository Ruins File:Shimotsuke Kokubunniji-ato, chumon-1.jpg|Middle Gate Ruins </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins - Designated on March 3, 1921, with additions to the designated area on March 2, 2005, and March 10, 2015.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|463|Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins}}</ref> * Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins - Designated on April 9, 1965, with an addition on July 28, 1977.<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|485|Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}}</ref>
== Location == '''Addresses''' * Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins: {{Coord|36|23|06.21|N|139|48|21.95|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins}} * Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins: {{Coord|36|23|06.98|N|139|48|44.73|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|name=Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}}
'''Related Facilities''' * Tochigi Prefectural Fudoki Hill Museum ** Admission: 100 yen for adults, 50 yen for high school and university students ** Hours: 9:00 AM–5:00 PM (last admission at 4:30 PM) ** Closed: Mondays (open on holidays and Prefectural Day), the day after holidays (open on weekends), year-end and New Year holidays
'''Access''' * Train: [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] ([[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]]) [[:en:Utsunomiya Line|Utsunomiya Line]] ([[:en:Tōhoku Main Line|Tōhoku Main Line]]) [[:en:Koganei Station|Koganei Station]] - Approximately 3.5 km ** Bicycle rentals available at the station
'''Nearby''' * [[:en:Shimotsuke Kokubun-ji|Shimotsuke Kokubunji]] - Successor temple. * Kokubun Kofun Cluster - Includes {{ill|Kabutozuka Kofun|ja|甲塚古墳 (下野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutozuka Kofun|qq=}}, Sannōzuka Kofun, Atago Shrine Kofun, and Marutsuka Kofun. * [[:en:Shimotsuke_Provincial_Capital|Shimotsuke Provincial Capital]]
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== References == * On-site explanatory boards * {{Cite book|title=Historical Place Names of Tochigi Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|chapter=Shimotsuke Kokubunji Ruins and Shimotsuke Kokubun-niji Ruins}}
